items.title: The Food (Star Anise from Third Countries) (Emergency Control) (Scotland) Order 2002
items.description: This Order is made under section 13(1) of the Food Safety Act 1990 in consequence of it appearing to the Scottish Ministers that the importation into Scotland of star anise consigned from certain third countries which are not member States of the European Community may involve imminent risk of injury to health.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 2) (Scotland) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 12) (Scotland) Partial Revocation Order 2002
items.description: This Order partially revokes the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 12) (Scotland) Order 2001 (“the principal Order”) which prohibited–

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 2) (Scotland) Partial Revocation Order 2002
items.description: This Order is the second partial revocation of the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 2) (Scotland) Order 2001 (“the principal Order”) which prohibited–

items.title: The Mobility and Access Committee for Scotland Regulations 2002
items.description: These regulations, which are made under sections 79 and 81(2) of the Transport (Scotland) Act 2001 establish a committee, to be known as the Mobility and Access Committee for Scotland. The committee will consider such matters relating to the needs of disabled persons in connection with transport and also give advice to the Scottish Ministers in relation to such matters as the committee thinks appropriate.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Amendment of Ordinary Cause Rules and Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules) (Applications under the Mortgage Rights (Scotland) Act 2001) 2002
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Ordinary Cause Rules and the Summary Applications Rules so as to regulate proceedings in which an order under section 2 of the Mortgage Rights (Scotland) Act 2001 (“the 2001 Act”) may be applied for by a defender (being applications under section 24 of the Conveyancing and Feudal Reform (Scotland) Act 1970 and proceedings under section 5 of the Heritable Securities (Scotland) Act 1894).

items.title: The Local Government Finance (Scotland) Order 2002
items.description: This Order–

items.title: The Standards in Scotland’s Schools etc. Act 2000 (Commencement No. 5) Order 2002
items.description: Article 3(1) of this Order brings into force sections 32, 33, 34, 35, 36 and 37 of the Standards in Scotland’s Schools etc. Act 2000 (“the Act”) on 26th February 2002.

items.title: The School Education (Amendment) (Scotland) Act 2002 (Commencement) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings into force the School Education (Amendment) (Scotland) Act 2002. 26th February 2002 is the appointed day for section 1 which amends the law on placing requests for children under school age.

items.title: The Health and Social Care Act 2001 (Commencement No. 9) (Scotland) Order 2002
items.description: This Order is the first Commencement Order made by the Scottish Ministers under the Health and Social Care Act 2001 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Preserved Rights (Transfer to Responsible Authorities) (Scotland) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, make the following provisions:–

items.title: The Public Finance and Accountability (Scotland) Act 2000 (Economy, efficiency and effectiveness examinations) (Specified bodies etc.) Order 2002
items.description: This Order specifies various bodies or office-holders, and classes of bodies or office-holders, in relation to which the Auditor General for Scotland may initiate examinations under section 23(1) of the Public Finance and Accountability (Scotland) Act 2000 into the economy, efficiency and effectiveness of the use of their resources in discharging their functions.

items.title: The Public Finance and Accountability (Scotland) Act 2000 (Access to Documents and Information) (Relevant Persons) Order 2002
items.description: This order specifies persons and classes of person in relation to whom the Auditor General for Scotland is entitled to have access to documents or information for the purposes of conducting an audit or an economy, efficiency and effectiveness examination under the Public Finance and Accountability (Scotland) Act 2000.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 3) (Scotland) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Conservation Measures) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Conservation Measures) (Scotland) Order 2000 (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) (Orkney) (No. 3) (Scotland) Revocation Order 2002
items.description: This Order revokes the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) (Orkney) (No. 3) (Scotland) Order 2001 (“the principal Order”) which prohibited–

items.title: The Financial Assistance for Environmental Purposes (Scotland) Order 2002
items.description: This Order varies section 153(1) of the Environment Protection Act 1990 so as to enable the Scottish Ministers to give financial assistance for the purposes of storing or treating any material or product prior to its disposal in an environmentally safe manner.

items.title: The Police and Fire Services (Finance) (Scotland) Act 2001 (Commencement) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings the provisions of the Police and Fire Services (Finance) (Scotland) Act 2001 (“the 2001 Act”), in so far as not already in force, into force on 11th March 2002. The 2001 Act makes provision about the carrying forward by police authorities, joint police boards and joint fire boards of unspent balances from one financial year to the next.

items.title: The National Assistance (Sums for Personal Requirements) (Scotland) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations set out the weekly sum which local authorities in Scotland are to assume, in the absence of special circumstances, that residents in accommodation arranged under the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 or section 7 of the Mental Health (Scotland) Act 1984 will need for their personal requirements.

items.title: The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) and (General Ophthalmic Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Regulations 1998 (“the 1998 Regulations”) which provide for payments to be made by means of a voucher system, in respect of eligibility to obtain a voucher and of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances. They also amend the National Health Service (General Ophthalmic Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”), which provide for arrangements under which ophthalmic medical practitioners and ophthalmic opticians provide general ophthalmic services under the National Health Service.

items.title: The Poultry Meat, Farmed Game Bird Meat and Rabbit Meat (Hygiene and Inspection) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Poultry Meat, Farmed Game Bird Meat and Rabbit Meat (Hygiene and Inspection) Regulations 1995 (“the principal Regulations”). These Regulations extend to Scotland only. The amendments have effect to–

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) Regulations 1996 so as to add certain proceedings under Part 6 (intervention orders and guardianship orders) of the Adults with Incapacity (Scotland) Act 2000 (asp 4) to the list of proceedings in regulation 14A of those Regulations (regulation 2). The availability of civil legal aid for those proceedings is based on the resources of the incapable adult, not the applicant, where the applicant is a person claiming or having an interest in the property, financial affairs or personal welfare of that adult.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rate (Scotland) Order 2002
items.description: This Order prescribes a rate of 47.8 pence in the pound as the non-domestic rate to be levied throughout Scotland in respect of financial year 2002–2003.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 9) (Scotland) Revocation Order 2002
items.description: This Order revokes the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 9) (Scotland) Order 2001 (“the principal Order”) which prohibited–

items.title: The Provision of School Education for Children under School Age (Prescribed Children) (Scotland) Order 2002
items.description: Section 32 of the Standard in Scotland’s Schools Act 2000 amends section 1 of the Education (Scotland) Act 1980 (“the 1980 Act”) by imposing a duty on an education authority to secure the provision of school education for categories of children as may be prescribed by the Scottish Ministers. Sub-sections (1A) and (1B) of the 1980 Act provide the power to describe the categories of eligible children and the amount of school education they are to receive.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Levying) (Scotland) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the amount payable in certain circumstances as non-domestic rates in respect of property in Scotland. They apply only to financial year 2002-2003 (“the relevant year”).

items.title: The Electricity from Non-Fossil Fuel Sources (Locational Flexibility) (Scotland) Order 2002
items.description: This Order modifies section 33 of the Electricity Act 1989 as it has effect in Scotland. It relates to arrangements made by public electricity suppliers under section 32 of the Electricity Act 1989 (as originally enacted) relating to the securing of generating capacity from non-fossil fuel generating stations. The Order allows such arrangements to continue to attract the fossil fuel levy in circumstances where the location of the generating station has changed.

items.title: The Electricity from Non-Fossil Fuel Sources (Scotland) Saving Arrangements (Modification) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Electricity from Non-Fossil Fuel Sources (Scotland) Saving Arrangements Order 2001 (S.I. 2001/3269).

items.title: The Fossil Fuel Levy (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Fossil Fuel Levy (Scotland) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/293).

items.title: The Adults with Incapacity (Supervision of Welfare Guardians etc. by Local Authorities) (Scotland) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the duties of the local authority in relation to supervision of welfare guardians and persons authorised under intervention orders for the purposes of section 10 of the Adults with Incapacity (Scotland) Act 2000 (regulation 2).

items.title: The Adults with Incapacity (Reports in Relation to Guardianship and Intervention Orders) (Scotland) Regulations 2002
items.description: These regulations prescribe the forms to be used by certain persons when completing reports for the purposes of applications for guardianship orders, renewal of guardianship orders and intervention orders under the Adults with Incapacity Act 2000.

items.title: The Adults with Incapacity (Recall of Guardians' Powers) (Scotland) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the forms to be completed in relation to applications to, and decisions of, the Mental Welfare Commission and local authorities in relation to recall of the powers of guardians under section 73 of the Adults with Incapacity (Scotland) Act 2000 (regulations 3 to 5, 7, 8, 10 and 11 and Schedules 1 to 9).

items.title: The Adults with Incapacity (Non-compliance with Decisions of Welfare Guardians) (Scotland) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the form of applications for orders or warrants under section 70 (non-compliance with decisions of guardian with welfare powers) of the Adults with Incapacity (Scotland) Act 2000 (“the Act”) (regulation 2 and Schedule).

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services and Dental Charges) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1996 (“the 1996 Regulations”), which provide for the arrangements under which dentists provide general dental services as part of the National Health Service (“NHS”) in Scotland. They also amend the National Health Service (Dental Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”), which provide for the making and recovery of charges for dental appliances supplied or repaired under the national health service, and for other dental treatment provided as part of NHS general dental services.

items.title: Greenham and Crookham Commons Act 2002
items.description: An Act to restore land at and in the vicinity of the Greenham and Crookham Commons as common land open to the public; to make provision for the conservation of the natural beauty of that land; to grant public access over that land in perpetuity and to make provision with respect to that public access; to restore and extend commoners' rights over that land; to constitute the Greenham and Crookham Common Commission for the management of that land; to confer powers on the West Berkshire District Council and on that Commission with respect to that land; and for connected and other purposes.

items.title: Land at Palace Avenue, Kensington (Acquisition of Freehold) Act 2002
items.description: An Act to authorise the trustees of the Imperial Tobacco Pension Fund to acquire the freehold of land forming part of the Royal Garden Hotel, Kensington; and for connected purposes.

items.title: HSBC Investment Banking Act 2002
items.description: An Act to provide for the transfer of the investment banking business of HSBC Investment Bank plc to HSBC Bank plc and the transfer of the private banking business of HSBC Investment Bank plc to HSBC Republic Bank (UK) Limited; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Barclays Group Reorganisation Act 2002
items.description: An Act to provide for the reorganisation of the undertakings of Barclays Bank PLC and certain of its subsidiaries; and for related purposes.

items.title: Milford Haven Port Authority Act 2002
items.description: An Act to alter the constitution of and confer further powers upon the Milford Haven Port Authority.

items.title: City of London (Ward Elections) Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make further provision with respect to the qualification of voters at ward elections in the city of London; and for connected purposes.

items.title: International Development Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision relating to the provision of assistance for countries outside the United Kingdom; to make provision with respect to certain international financial institutions and the Commonwealth Scholarship Commission; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Consolidated Fund Act 2002 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to authorise the use of resources for the service of the year ending on 31st March 2002 and to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the years ending on 31st March 2001 and 2002.

items.title: Office of Communications Act 2002
items.description: An Act to establish a body corporate to be known as the Office of Communications; and to confer functions in relation to proposals about the regulation of communications on that body, on certain existing regulators and on the Secretary of State.

items.title: Football (Disorder) (Amendment) Act 2002 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to amend section 5 of the Football (Disorder) Act 2000; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Electoral Fraud (Northern Ireland) Act 2002
items.description: An Act to provide for the supply to the Chief Electoral Officer for Northern Ireland of the signatures, dates of birth and national insurance numbers of electors and persons seeking registration as electors in Northern Ireland and of information relating to their period of residence in Northern Ireland and addresses in respect of which they are or have applied to be registered; for the use of that information in connection with elections in Northern Ireland; for the issue of electoral identity cards by the Chief Electoral Officer for Northern Ireland; for the modification in relation to voters with disabilities of certain rules about voting procedure in Northern Ireland; and for connected purposes.

items.title: National Heritage Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make further provision in relation to the functions of the Historic Buildings and Monuments Commission for England; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Commonhold and Leasehold Reform Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision about commonhold land and to amend the law about leasehold property.

items.title: State Pension Credit Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision for and in connection with a new social security benefit called state pension credit; and to amend section 47(1) of the Pension Schemes Act 1993.

items.title: National Health Service Reform and Health Care Professions Act 2002
items.description: An Act to amend the law about the national health service; to establish and make provision in connection with a Commission for Patient and Public Involvement in Health; to make provision in relation to arrangements for joint working between NHS bodies and the prison service, and between NHS bodies and local authorities in Wales; to make provision in connection with the regulation of health care professions; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Appropriation Act 2002 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to authorise the use of resources and the issue of sums out of the Consolidated Fund for the service of the year ending on 31st March 2003; to appropriate the supply authorised in this Session of Parliament; and to repeal certain Consolidated Fund and Appropriation Acts.

items.title: National Insurance Contributions Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision for, and in connection with, increasing national insurance contributions and for applying the increases towards the cost of the national health service.

items.title: Sex Discrimination (Election Candidates) Act 2002
items.description: An Act to exclude from the operation of the Sex Discrimination Act 1975 and the Sex Discrimination (Northern Ireland) Order 1976 certain matters relating to the selection of candidates by political parties.

items.title: Industrial and Provident Societies Act 2002 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to enable the law relating to societies registered under the Industrial and Provident Societies Act 1965 to be amended so as to bring it into conformity with certain aspects of the law relating to companies; to amend the procedure whereby such a society may convert itself into, or amalgamate with or transfer its engagements to, a company; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Tax Credits Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision for tax credits; to amend the law about child benefit and guardian’s allowance; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Employment Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision for statutory rights to paternity and adoption leave and pay; to amend the law relating to statutory maternity leave and pay; to amend the Employment Tribunals Act 1996; to make provision for the use of statutory procedures in relation to employment disputes; to amend the law relating to particulars of employment; to make provision about compromise agreements; to make provision for questionnaires in relation to equal pay; to make provision in connection with trade union learning representatives; to amend section 110 of the Employment Rights Act 1996; to make provision about fixed-term work; to make provision about flexible working; to amend the law relating to maternity allowance; to make provision for work-focused interviews for partners of benefit claimants; to make provision about the use of information for, or relating to, employment and training; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Finance Act 2002
items.description: An Act to grant certain duties, to alter other duties, and to amend the law relating to the National Debt and the Public Revenue, and to make further provision in connection with finance.

items.title: European Parliamentary Elections Act 2002 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to consolidate the European Parliamentary Elections Acts 1978, 1993 and 1999.

items.title: Copyright, etc. and Trade Marks (Offences and Enforcement) Act 2002
items.description: An Act to amend the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 in respect of criminal offences, search warrants, powers of seizure and orders for forfeiture; to amend the Trade Marks Act 1994 in respect of search warrants and powers of seizure; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Justice (Northern Ireland) Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision about the judiciary in Northern Ireland and to amend section 6 of the Appellate Jurisdiction Act 1876; to make provision about the law officers and other legal officers and the courts in Northern Ireland; to establish a Public Prosecution Service for Northern Ireland, a Chief Inspector of Criminal Justice in Northern Ireland and a Northern Ireland Law Commission; to amend the law of youth justice in Northern Ireland; to make provision for making available to victims of crime information about the release of offenders in Northern Ireland; to make provision about community safety in Northern Ireland; to amend the law of legal aid in Northern Ireland; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Divorce (Religious Marriages) Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision enabling a court to require the dissolution of a religious marriage before granting a civil divorce.

items.title: Export Control Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision enabling controls to be imposed on the exportation of goods, the transfer of technology, the provision of technical assistance overseas and activities connected with trade in controlled goods; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Proceeds of Crime Act 2002
items.description: An Act to establish the Assets Recovery Agency and make provision about the appointment of its Director and his functions (including Revenue functions), to provide for confiscation orders in relation to persons who benefit from criminal conduct and for restraint orders to prohibit dealing with property, to allow the recovery of property which is or represents property obtained through unlawful conduct or which is intended to be used in unlawful conduct, to make provision about money laundering, to make provision about investigations relating to benefit from criminal conduct or to property which is or represents property obtained through unlawful conduct or to money laundering, to make provision to give effect to overseas requests and orders made where property is found or believed to be obtained through criminal conduct, and for connected purposes.

items.title: European Communities (Amendment) Act 2002 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make provision consequential on the Treaty signed at Nice on 26th February 2001 amending the Treaty on European Union, the Treaties establishing the European Communities and certain related Acts.

items.title: Police Reform Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make new provision about the supervision, administration, functions and conduct of police forces, police officers and other persons serving with, or carrying out functions in relation to, the police; to amend police powers and to provide for the exercise of police powers by persons who are not police officers; to amend the law relating to anti-social behaviour orders; to amend the law relating to sex offender orders; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Mobile Telephones (Re-programming) Act 2002
items.description: An Act to create offences in respect of unique electronic equipment identifiers of mobile wireless communications devices.

items.title: Education Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision about education, training and childcare.

items.title: Copyright (Visually Impaired Persons) Act 2002 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to permit, without infringement of copyright, the transfer of copyright works to formats accessible to visually impaired persons.

items.title: Employee Share Schemes Act 2002 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make provision relating to employee share schemes; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Public Trustee (Liability and Fees) Act 2002
items.description: An Act to amend the Public Trustee Act 1906 in respect of the liability and fees of the Public Trustee; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Tobacco Advertising and Promotion Act 2002
items.description: An Act to control the advertising and promotion of tobacco products; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Private Hire Vehicles (Carriage of Guide Dogs etc.) Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision for the carriage of disabled persons accompanied by guide dogs, hearing dogs or other assistance dogs by drivers and operators of private hire vehicles; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Adoption and Children Act 2002
items.description: An Act to restate and amend the law relating to adoption; to make further amendments of the law relating to children; to amend section 93 of the Local Government Act 2000; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Commonwealth Act 2002
items.description: An Act to amend the law with respect to the Commonwealth Institute; to make provision in connection with the admission of Cameroon and Mozambique to the Commonwealth; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Travel Concessions (Eligibility) Act 2002
items.description: An Act to amend the law relating to the age at which certain persons become eligible to receive travel concessions on journeys on public passenger transport services; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Enterprise Act 2002
items.description: An act to establish and provide for the functions of the Office of Fair Trading, the Competition Appeal Tribunal and the Competition Service; to make provision about mergers and market structures and conduct; to amend the constitution and functions of the Competition Commission; to create an offence for those entering into certain anti-competitive agreements; to provide for the disqualification of directors of companies engaging in certain anti-competitive practices; to make other provision about competition law; to amend the law relating to the protection of the collective interests of consumers; to make further provision about the disclosure of information obtained under competition and consumer legislation; to amend the Insolvency Act 1986 and make other provision about insolvency; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Nationality, Immigration and Asylum Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision about nationality, immigration and asylum; to create offences in connection with international traffic in prostitution; to make provision about international projects connected with migration; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Animal Health Act 2002
items.description: An Act to amend the Animal Health Act 1981.

items.title: Consolidated Fund (No. 2) Act 2002 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to authorise the use of resources for the service of the years ending on 31st March 2003 and 2004 and to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the years ending on 31st March 2003 and 2004.

items.title: Appropriation (No. 2) Act 2002 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to modify limits on non-operating appropriations in aid set for the year that ended with 31st March 2002.

items.title: Civil Defence (Grant) Act 2002 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to replace section 3 of the Civil Defence Act 1948 in so far as it applies to authorities in England or Wales.

items.title: Northern Ireland Arms Decommissioning (Amendment) Act 2002
items.description: An Act to provide for the extension of the amnesty period fixed by section 2 of the Northern Ireland Arms Decommissioning Act 1997.

items.title: Homelessness Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make further provision about the functions of local housing authorities relating to homelessness and the allocation of housing accommodation; and for connected purposes.

items.title: British Overseas Territories Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision about the name “British overseas territories” and British citizenship so far as relating to the British overseas territories.

items.title: Land Registration Act 2002
items.description: An Act to make provision about land registration; and for connected purposes.

items.title: The Landfill Tax (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st February 2002, amend the Landfill Tax Regulations 1996 to provide that the temporary holding of material pending its use for site restoration purposes at the landfill site at which the disposal was made, or pending it being sorted, is not a taxable disposal. They also correct a drafting error.

items.title: Employment Rights (Increase of Limits) Order 2002 (revoked)
items.description: This Order increases, from 1st February 2002, the limits applying to certain awards of employment tribunals, and other amounts payable under employment legislation, as specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Camden Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Camden Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2) and the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Lewisham Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Lewisham Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2) and the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Islington Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Islington Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2) and the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Southwark Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Southwark Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2) and the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Kensington and Chelsea Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Kensington and Chelsea Primary Care Trust (“ the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2) and the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Brent Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Brent Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2) and the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Westminster Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Westminster Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2) and the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Croydon Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Croydon Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2) and the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Teddington, Twickenham and Hamptons Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment and Change of Name Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the establishment Order of the Teddington, Twickenham and Hamptons Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977 by increasing from 5 to 6 the number of officer members, and by increasing from 5 to 6 the number of non-officer members.

items.title: The Nelson and West Merton Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment and Change of Name Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the establishment order of the Nelson and West Merton Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977.

items.title: The Harrow Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Harrow Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2) and the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Pet Travel Scheme (Pilot Arrangements) (England) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Pet Travel Scheme (Pilot Arrangements) (England) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/3443, as amended by S.I. 2000/1298, S.I. 2000/1564, S.I. 2000/1641 and S.I. 2001/6) so as to extend the scheme to Bahrain as a rabies-free island.

items.title: The Transport Act 2000 (Commencement No. 9 and Transitional Provisions) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st May 2002 the following provisions of the Transport Act 2000—

items.title: The Bus Service Operators Grant (England) Regulations 2002
items.description: These regulations describe the “eligible bus services” for the purposes of section 154 of the Transport Act 2000 (“the 2000 Act”). Under that section grants may be made to operators of eligible bus services towards their costs in operating the service. These Regulations re-enact the previous eligibility rules for fuel duty rebates under section 92 of the Finance Act 1965, which is superseded by section 154 of the 2000 Act. But these Regulations (regulation 3(1)(c) and (4)) extend eligibility to services provided by a range of non-profit making community transport bodies whose services do not follow a fixed route or timetable and are for use by particular categories of passengers, rather than the general public.

items.title: Travel Concessions (Eligible Services) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the Travel Concessions (Eligible Services) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A303 Trunk Road (Folly Bottom Junction) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A303 Trunk Road (Folly Bottom Junction) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A303 Trunk Road (Folly Bottom Junction Improvement Slip Roads) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A303 Trunk Road (Folly Bottom Junction Improvement Slip Roads) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Borough of South Ribble (Electoral Changes) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Borough of South Ribble (Electoral Changes) Order 2001, in order to give full effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England by correcting an error in relation to the parish wards of the parish of Penwortham.

items.title: The District of Craven (Ribble Banks Parish Council) (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order makes changes to the arrangements for the elections for parish councillors for the Ribble Banks Parish Council that are consequential upon electoral changes made in relation to the District of Craven by the District of Craven (Electoral Changes) Order 2000. These changes are designed to bring the dates of elections of parish councillors into line with the dates of election for councillors for the district ward of Settle and Ribble Banks, into which now fall the parishes represented by councillors elected to the Ribble Banks Parish Council. Those parishes are the parishes of Halton Rest, Rathmell and Wigglesworth.

items.title: The District of East Riding (Electoral Changes) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The District of East Riding (Electoral Changes) (Amendment) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Borough of Milton Keynes (Electoral Changes) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Borough of Milton Keynes (Electoral Changes) Order 2001, in order to give full effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England by correcting an error in relation to the date of commencement of articles 4 and 5 of the 2001 Order.

items.title: The District of Forest of Dean (Electoral Changes) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order substitutes a new map for that originally prepared in relation to the District of Forest of Dean (Electoral Changes) Order 2001, in order to give full effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the District of Forest of Dean.

items.title: The District of Babergh (Electoral Changes) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: In order to give full effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the District of Babergh, this Order corrects errors in the Schedule to the District of Babergh (Electoral Changes) Order 2001.

items.title: The Insolvency Act 1986 (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Insolvency Act 1986 (c. 45) to extend existing rule-making powers under the Act to allow for making rules which are required as a result of the adoption by the Council of the European Union of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1346/2000 of 29th May 2000 on insolvency proceedings, Official Journal No. L160, 30.06.00. p.1 (the “Regulation”) which comes into force on 31st May 2002. As a result of making these amendments, rules made under the amended sections 411 and 412 of the Insolvency Act 1986 in relation to the Regulation, will, like rules presently made under the Act, need to be reviewed by the Insolvency Rules Committee. The Insolvency Rules Committee is consulted by the Lord Chancellor prior to making insolvency rules and consists of judges and practising insolvency professionals (specified in section 413).

items.title: The A282 Trunk Road (Dartford–Thurrock Crossing Charging Scheme) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A282 Trunk Road (Dartford–Thurrock Crossing Charging Scheme) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Warrant Enforcement Staff Pensions Order 2002
items.description: This Order provides for warrant enforcement staff to continue to be eligible to be members of the Metropolitan Civil Staffs Superannuation Scheme despite their ceasing to be members of the metropolitan civil staffs as a result of the transfer of their employment to the Greater London Magistrates' Courts Authority. The Order has retrospective effect as permitted by section 411(7) of the Greater London Authority Act 1999.

items.title: The Greater London Authority (Declaration of Acceptance of Office) Order 2002
items.description: This Order prescribes the form of declaration of office for the Mayor of London, members of the London Assembly and any acting Mayor of London in accordance with section 28 of Greater London Authority Act 1999. The declaration must be made within two months of assumption of office.

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), as substituted and amended, with respect to the standard rate of interest applicable to a loan which qualifies for income support under that Schedule to those Regulations. The new rate is 5.74 per cent. (regulation 2). The Regulations also revoke, with a saving provision, regulations which made the previous amendment to that standard rate of interest (regulation 3).

items.title: The Local Authorities (Executive Arrangements) (Modification of Enactments) (England) Order 2002
items.description: This Order modifies primary and secondary legislation for the purposes of, in consequence of, or for giving full effect to, provisions in Part II of the Local Government Act 2000 (“the 2000 Act”) (arrangements with respect to executives etc.). This Order and the modifications made by it apply to England only.

items.title: The A57 Trunk Road (A57/M60/M67 Denton Roundabout to Manchester City Boundary) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A57 Trunk Road (A57/M60/M67 Denton Roundabout to Manchester City Boundary) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Regulatory Reform (Golden Jubilee Licensing) Order 2002
items.description: This Order is made under section 1 of the Regulatory Reform Act 2001. It reforms the law relating to licensing hours which has the effect of imposing burdens on people carrying out certain activities with a view to reducing those burdens on the occasion of Her Majesty’s Golden Jubilee. It does so by amending provisions in the Regulatory Reform (Special Occasions Licensing) Order 2001 to allow the sale of intoxicating liquor in licensed premises other than off-licences, in registered clubs and in licensed canteens in the period between what would otherwise be the end of the permitted hours on 3rd June 2002 and 1 am on 4th June 2002.

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) Order 2002
items.description: This order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, safety zones having a radius of 500 metres from the specified point around each installation specified in the Schedule to this order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)) (article 2).

items.title: The Heathrow Express Railway Extension Order 2002
items.description: This Order authorises Heathrow Airport Limited to construct works and compulsorily to acquire and use land for the purpose of making an extension to the Heathrow Express Railway from the Central Terminal Area of Heathrow Airport to the proposed site of the new Terminal 5 building. This Order also confers ancillary powers in connection with the construction and operation of that extension.

items.title: The Piccadilly Line (Heathrow T5 Extension) Order 2002
items.description: This Order authorises London Underground Limited to construct works and compulsorily to acquire land for the purpose of making an extension to its Piccadilly Line from the Central Terminal Area of Heathrow Airport to the proposed site of the new Terminal 5 building. The Order also confers ancillary powers in connection with the construction and operation of that extension including the power to enter into agreements with Heathrow Airport Limited.

items.title: The Docklands Light Railway (Silvertown and London City Airport Extension) Order 2002
items.description: This Order authorises Docklands Light Railway Limited (DLRL) to construct an extension to the Docklands Light Railway from Canning Town in the London Borough of Newham to North Woolwich in the London Borough of Newham, passing through Silvertown and alongside London City Airport and, for that purpose, compulsorily or by agreement to acquire land and rights in land.

items.title: The Plant Health (England) (Amendment) Order 2002 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the Plant Health (Great Britain) Order 1993 (“the principal Order”) so as to implement certain elements of Commission Directives 2002/29/EC (OJ No. L 77, 20.3.02, p. 26) and 2002/28/EC (OJ No. L 77, 20.3.02, p. 23) in England which are not implemented in other legislation. Directive 2002/29/EC amends the protected zones established under Council Directive 2000/29/EC (OJ No. L169, 10.7.00, p.1) (“the principal Plant Health Directive”) and scheduled in the Annex to Commission Directive 2001/32/EC (OJ No. L127, 9.5.01, p. 38). Directive 2002/28/EC provides for consequential amendments to certain Annexes of the principal Plant Health Directive.

items.title: The Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 (Visual Recording of Interviews) (Certain Police Areas) Order 2002
items.description: This Order requires the visual recording of interviews held by police officers at Basingstoke, Portsmouth, Southampton, Chatham, Gravesend, Tonbridge, Bromley, Colindale, Edmonton, Redditch, Telford and Worcester police stations.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Fibernet UK Limited) Order 2002
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Fibernet UK Limited a licence on 12 March 2002 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 18th April 2002. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Fibernet UK Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 17th May 2002.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Eurocall Limited) Order 2002
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Eurocall Limited a licence on 12 March 2002 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 18th April 2002. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Eurocall Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 17th May 2002.

items.title: The Vehicle Excise Duty (Designation of Small Islands) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: The Order amends the Vehicle Excise Duty (Designation of Small Islands) Order 1995 and designates Shapinsay, Orkney Mainland, Harris and Lewis and Shetland Mainland as small islands for the purposes of paragraph 18 of Schedule 1 to the Vehicle Excise and Registration Act 1994, which defines the expression “island goods vehicle” for the purposes of Part VIII of that Schedule.

items.title: The National Health Service (Clinical Negligence Scheme) Amendment Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Clinical Negligence Scheme) Regulations 1996 which established a Scheme whereby NHS trusts and certain other bodies providing NHS services may make provision for meeting liabilities to third parties in connection with personal injury arising out of negligence in the carrying out of their functions.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 18th April 2002, further amend the Value Added Tax Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/2518) (“the principal Regulations”) in order to counter avoidance schemes based on the partial exemption method specified in regulation 101 of the principal Regulations and to deal with situations where the result of the method is clearly unreasonable.

items.title: The Geneva Conventions (Amendment) Act (Overseas Territories) Order 2002
items.description: This Order extends to the Territories specified in Schedule 1 the Geneva Conventions (Amendment) Act 1995, subject to the exceptions and modifications specified in Schedule 2.

items.title: The Overseas Territories (Zimbabwe) (Restrictive Measures) Order 2002
items.description: This Order applies to each of the British overseas territories listed in Schedule 1. It imposes restrictive measures in respect of Zimbabwe and those who bear a wide responsibility for the serious violations of human rights and of the freedom of opinion, of association and of peaceful assembly in that country. These measures include the prohibition of the delivery or supply of arms and related matériel and equipment that might be used for internal repression to Zimbabwe, and the prohibition of making available funds, financial assets or economic resources to persons listed in Schedule 4 and the freezing of their funds, financial assets or economic resources.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Jersey) (Amendment No. 2) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Air Navigation (Jersey) Order 2000 that extended the Air Navigation (No. 2) Order 1995 to Jersey. A further modification is added to those contained in the Schedule to the 2000 Order to alter the Approach Radar Control Rating. The effect is that approach control services may be provided, with the aid of surveillance radar equipment, in an area within or outside the Channel Islands Control Zone in accordance with any agreement made by the relevant authorities in the United Kingdom or France, and Jersey.

items.title: The Education (Inspectors of Education and Training in Wales) Order 2002
items.description: This Order appoints the persons named in the Schedule Her Majesty’s Inspectors of Education and Training in Wales.

items.title: The Merger Report (Interbrew SA and Bass PLC) (Interim Provision) (Revocation) Order 2002
items.description: This Order revokes the Merger Report (Interbrew SA and Bass PLC) (Interim Provision) Order 2001 (“the 2001 Order”) which prohibited Interbrew SA, Interbrew UK Limited and any other member of the Interbrew group and Bass Holdings Limited from doing things which might have impeded the operation of a further order which the Secretary of State had under consideration for the purpose of remedying or preventing the adverse effects specified in a report of the Competition Commission on the acquisition by Interbrew SA of the brewing interests of Bass PLC (Cm 5019, obtainable from Her Majesty’s Stationery Office).

items.title: The European Communities (Designation) (No. 2) Order 2002 (revoked)
items.description: This is a further Order in the series designating authorities which may exercise the power to make regulations conferred by section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 and specifying the matters in relation to which that power may be exercised.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Acquire) (Discount) Order 2002
items.description: Tenants of registered social landlords who have a right to acquire their homes under sections 16 and 17 of the Housing Act 1996 are able to do so with a discount from the purchase price. This Order specifies for local authority areas in England the amount of discount to be given on the exercise of that right. The amount of the discount varies according to the area in which the dwelling is situated. In any event, the maximum discount a tenant may receive is limited by this Order to 50% of the market value of the dwelling.

items.title: The Radioactive Material (Road Transport) (Definition of Radioactive Material) Order 2002
items.description: Section 1(1)(b) of the Radioactive Material (Road Transport) Act 1991 provides that, in that Act, “radioactive material” means any material having a specific activity in excess of 70 kilobecquerels per kilogram or such lesser specific activity as is specified in an Order. This Order specifies the level of activity at 0.1 kilobecquerels per kilogram, thus lowering it from 70 kilobecquerels per kilogram and widening the definition of “radioactive material” for the purposes of that Act.

items.title: The Radioactive Material (Road Transport) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement regulations made by the International Atomic Energy Agency (“IAEA”) in its safety standards series for the Safe Transport of Radioactive Material (1996 Edition, No. TS-R-1) and additional requirements contained in the European Agreement concerning the international carriage of dangerous goods by road (ADR) as amended up to 1 July 2001. The Regulations also implement as respects Great Britain provisions of Council Directive 96/29/EURATOM (OJ No. L159, 29.6.96, p.1) and Commission Directive 2001/7/EC (OJ L30, 1.2.01, p.43).

items.title: The Government of Further Education Corporations (Revocation) (England) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations revoke the regulations listed in Part 1 of the Schedule and the regulations listed in Part 2 of the Schedule in relation to England. The regulations revoke prescribed initial instruments and articles of government for further education corporations. All the initial instruments and articles of government for further education corporations in England have since been replaced by orders under section 22 of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992 (as amended by paragraph 23 of Schedule 9 to the Learning and Skills Act 2000 (c. 21)). Consequently the regulations listed in Part 1 of the Schedule have ceased to have effect and the regulations listed in Part 2 of the Schedule have ceased to have effect in relation to England.

items.title: The Health and Social Care Act 2001 (Commencement No. 8) Order 2002
items.description: This Order is the eighth Commencement Order made under the Health and Social Care Act 2001 (“the 2001 Act”) and brings into force certain provisions of that Act.

items.title: The Criminal Justice and Police Act 2001 (Commencement No. 6) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings into force section 130 of the Criminal Justice and Police Act 2001 (remands and committals to secure accommodation etc) on 22nd April 2002 for the eleven areas listed in the Schedule to this Order and on 16th September 2002 for all other areas.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Increase of Registration Limits) Order 2002
items.description: This Order increases the VAT registration limits for taxable supplies and for acquisitions from other member States from £54,000 to £55,000, with effect from 25th April 2002.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Consideration for Fuel Provided for Private Use) Order 2002
items.description: VAT is payable if road fuel of a business is used for private motoring. The VAT due must be calculated using flat-rate values related to engine type and size.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Rate of Discount for 2002/03) (England) Regulations 2002
items.description: Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 makes provision for the capital finance of local authorities. Section 49(2) sets out a formula for determining for the purposes of Part IV the value of the consideration falling to be given by a local authority under a credit arrangement in any financial year after the one in which the arrangement comes into being. The percentage rate of discount prescribed for the financial year is one of the factors referred to in the formula. For the financial year beginning on 1st April 2002 these Regulations prescribe 7.2 per cent which is 0.3 per cent less than the rate of discount prescribed for 2001/02.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Reduced Rate) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1 June 2002, amends Groups 3, 6 and 7 of Schedule 7A to the Value Added Tax Act 1994. This Schedule contains the Groups of supplies that are subject to the reduced rate of VAT of 5%.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Construction of Buildings) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends Note (17) to Group 5 of Schedule 8 to the Value Added Tax Act 1994.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Buildings and Land) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends Schedule 10 to the Value Added Tax Act 1994.

items.title: The Al-Qa'ida and Taliban (United Nations Measures) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, gives effect to Resolution 1390, adopted by the Security Council of the United Nations on 16th January 2002. In view of the changing situation in Afghanistan, Resolution 1390 continued, amended or terminated as appropriate certain provisions of Security Council Resolutions 1267, adopted on 15th October 1999, and 1333, adopted on 19th December 2000 which imposed sanctions in relation to Usama bin Laden, the Taliban and the territory of Afghanistan. The effect of Resolution 1390 is to require States to prohibit the delivery or supply of arms and related matériel and the provision of related technical assistance and training to Usama bin Laden, Al-Qa'ida, the Taliban and their associates as designated in a list by the Security Council. It also prohibits the making of funds available to those persons.

items.title: The Electricity and Gas (Determination of Turnover for Penalties) Order 2002
items.description: This Order makes provision for the determination of the turnover of a licence holder for the purposes of section 27A(8) of the Electricity Act 1989 (“the Electricity Act”) and section 30A(8) of the Gas Act 1986 (“the Gas Act”) (article 3).

items.title: The South Stoke Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the establishment order of the South Stoke Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977 by making changes to the area in which the trust is to undertake its functions.

items.title: The North Stoke Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the establishment order of the North Stoke Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977 by making changes to the area in which the trust is to undertake its functions.

items.title: The Burntwood, Lichfield and Tamworth Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the establishment order of the Burntwood, Lichfield and Tamworth Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977 by making changes to the area in which the trust is to undertake its functions.

items.title: The Rushmoor and Hart Primary Care Trust (Change of Name) No. 2 Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Establishment Order of the Rushmoor and Hart Primary Care Trust, a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977, by changing the name of that trust to Blackwater Valley and Hart Primary Care Trust, and makes consequential amendments to the Establishment Order and transitional provisions (article 2). The Order also revokes the Rushmoor and Hart Primary Care Trust (Change of Name) Order 2002 which had changed the name of the trust to Blackwater Valley Primary Care Trust.

items.title: The Central Derby Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Establishment Order of the Central Derby Primary Care Trust, a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977, by adding to the area of the trust the electoral ward of Blagreaves in the city of Derby.

items.title: The Tameside and Glossop Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Tameside and Glossop Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), and the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Torbay Primary Care Trust and the Teignbridge Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Establishment Orders of the Torbay Primary Care Trust and the Teignbridge Primary Care Trust, Primary Care Trusts established by Orders made under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It removes a restriction on the exercise of functions by these Primary Care Trusts so that they can provide services directly to patients.

items.title: The Eastleigh and Test Valley South Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Eastleigh and Test Valley South Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), and the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Al-Qa'ida and Taliban (United Nations Measures) (Overseas Territories) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, applies to each of the territories specified in Schedule 1. It gives effect to Resolution 1390, adopted by the Security Council of the United Nations on 16th January 2002. In view of the changing situation in Afghanistan, Resolution 1390 continued, amended or terminated as appropriate certain provisions of Security Council Resolutions 1267, adopted on 15th October 1999, and 1333, adopted on 19th December 2000 which imposed sanctions in relation to Usama bin Laden, the Taliban and the territory of Afghanistan. The effect of Resolution 1390 is to require States to prohibit the delivery or supply of arms and related matériel and the provision of related technical assistance and training to Usama bin Laden, Al-Qa'ida, the Taliban and their associates. It also prohibits the making of funds available to those persons.

items.title: The Fareham and Gosport Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Fareham and Gosport Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), and the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Fenland Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Establishment Order of the Fenland Primary Care Trust, a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977, by changing its name to East Cambridgeshire and Fenlands Primary Care Trust and substitutes a new schedule to reflect the increased area of the Primary Care Trust.

items.title: The Northumberland Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Northumberland Care Trust (“the trust”), a Primary Care Trust designated as a Care Trust as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977 and section 45 of the Health and Social Care Act 2001. It specifies the trust’s operational date as the day on which it is established (article 2) and specifies the area for which it is established (article 3).

items.title: The Maidstone and Malling Primary Care Trust Change of Name and (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Establishment Order of the Maidstone and Malling Primary Care Trust, a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977, by changing its name to Maidstone Weald Primary Care Trust and substitutes a new Schedule to reflect the increased area of the Primary Care Trust.

items.title: The Health Professions Order 2001 (Transitional Provisions) Order 2002
items.description: This Order makes transitional provisions under article 48(2) of the Health Professions Order 2001 (“the Order”) to enable consultation and other provisions in the Order to apply, pending the establishment of a register under the Order, to those who are on one of the registers maintained by the Health Professions Council under the transitional provisions in paragraph 11 of Schedule 2 to the Order.

items.title: The Nursing and Midwifery Order 2001 (Transitional Provisions) Order 2002
items.description: This Order makes transitional provisions under article 54(2) of the Nursing and Midwifery Order 2001 (“the Order”) to enable consultation and other provisions in the Order to apply, pending the establishment of a register under the Order, to those who are on the register maintained by the Nursing and Midwifery Council under the transitional provisions in paragraph 10 of Schedule 2 to the Order.

items.title: The Gaming Clubs (Bankers' Games) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: Section 13(1) of the Gaming Act 1968 prohibits the playing of bankers' games and games of unequal chance on premises licensed under that Act. Section 13(2), however, authorises the making of regulations to provide that this prohibition shall not have effect in relation to games specified in the Regulations, if so played as to comply with the Regulations.

items.title: The Social Security (Claims and Information) (Housing Benefit and Council Tax Benefit) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations permit the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions or persons providing services to him to collect information relating to housing benefit and council tax benefit and forward this information to the local authority.

items.title: The Bootle and Litherland Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Establishment Order of the Bootle and Litherland Primary Care Trust, a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977, by changing its name to South Sefton Primary Care Trust and substitutes a new Schedule for the Schedule to the Principal Order, to enlarge the area of the trust.

items.title: The Devon County Council (Barnstaple Downstream Bridge) Scheme 2000 Confirmation Instrument 2002
items.description: This version of the The Devon County Council (Barnstaple Downstream Bridge) Scheme 2000 Confirmation Instrument 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Extradition) (Amendment) Rules 2002
items.description: Rules 4 and 5 of these Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts (Extradition) Rules 1989 to provide a form of notice of consent to committal for return to be used for the purposes of section 14A(3) of the Extradition Act 1989 (“the Act”). The amendment made by rule 4 provides that a Senior District Judge (Chief Magistrate) or another District Judge (Magistrates' Courts) designated by him for the purposes of the Act may order the committal for return of a person who gives consent under section 14A of the Act before he is committed under section 9 of the Act.

items.title: The Education (Birmingham College of Food, Tourism and Creative Studies) (Transfer to the Higher Education Sector) Order 2002
items.description: This Order makes provision for the transfer of the Birmingham College of Food, Tourism and Creative Studies to the higher education sector. On 1st August 2002 that corporation becomes a higher education corporation.

items.title: The Education (Bursaries for School Teacher Training) (England) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Bursaries for School Teacher Training) (England) Regulations 2002 in two ways. The exclusion of fast track teachers from eligibility for bursaries and the requirement that to be eligible a teacher must have successfully completed an induction period are removed from those Regulations.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 25th April 2002, further amend the Value Added Tax Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/2518) (the “principal Regulations”). However, Part I of the Regulations, which amends the annual accounting scheme, has effect in the case of persons who are already operating the annual accounting scheme only from the beginning of their relevant accounting periods starting after 24th April.

items.title: The Football Spectators (World Cup Control Period) (No. 2) Order 2002
items.description: This Order describes the control period under the Football Spectators Act 1989 for the 2002 FIFA (Fédération Internationale de Football Associations) World Cup Korea/Japan. The control period begins on 26th May 2002, being five days before the first match in the tournament, and ends when the last match in the tournament is finished or cancelled. The last match is due to be played on 30th June 2002.

items.title: The Personal Protective Equipment Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Personal Protective Equipment (EC Directive) Regulations 1992, (S.I. 1992/3139; as amended by S.I. 1993/3074, S.I. 1994/2326 and S.I. 1996/3039 and extended by section 2(1) of the Economic Area Act 1993, c. 51).

items.title: The Immigration Appeals (Family Visitor) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations revoke the provisions of the Immigration Appeals (Family Visitor) (No. 2) Regulations 2000 which require payment, specify the form of payment, and provide for repayment of a fee by a family visitor when appealing against a refusal of entry clearance.

items.title: The Criminal Justice and Court Services Act 2000 (Commencement No. 9) Order 2002
items.description: This Order applies to England and Wales. It brings into force section 57 of the Criminal Justice and Court Services Act 2000 (drug testing persons in police detention) within certain police areas. The provision has already been commenced in the police areas of Nottinghamshire, Staffordshire and the metropolitan police district by virtue of S.I. 2001/2232.

items.title: The Home Energy Efficiency Scheme (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: The Home Energy Efficiency Scheme (England) Regulations 2000 (S.I. 2000/1280) enable the Secretary of State to make or arrange for the making of grant for the improvement of energy efficiency in dwellings occupied by persons on low incomes with children, elderly persons on low incomes or persons in receipt of benefit relating to ill health. These Regulations amend the 2000 Regulations. In addition to minor and drafting amendments the following changes of substance are made.

items.title: The Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 (Codes of Practice) (Modifications to Code C and Code D) (Certain Police Areas) Order 2002
items.description: Section 63B of the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 gives police officers a new power to undertake tests for the presence of specified Class A drugs in relation to certain persons in police detention. Section 66(2) of the 1984 Act provides that codes in connection with these powers must be in place before the powers are exercised. This Order puts in place modifications to the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 codes of practice to provide for drug testing within police detention. The modifications extend only to named police areas where the drug testing provisions are to be piloted. The provisions have already been piloted in the metropolitan police district, Nottinghamshire and Staffordshire by virtue of S.I. 2001/2254. They will continue to be used in those areas under this Order, which revokes S.I. 2001/2254, but will now also be introduced in Bedfordshire, Devon and Cornwall, Lancashire, Merseyside, South Yorkshire and North Wales.

items.title: The Atomic Energy Authority (Special Constables) Order 2002
items.description: This Order designates those licensed nuclear sites at which special constables appointed on the nomination of the United Kingdom Atomic Energy Authority are not to have the powers and privileges of constables.

items.title: The Climate Change Levy (General) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These regulations amend The Climate Change Levy (General) Regulations 2001 (S.I. 2001/838) so as to make provision for an annual accounting scheme.

items.title: The Planning and Compensation Act 1991 (Amendment of Schedule 18) (England) Order 2002
items.description: Part I of Schedule 18 to the Planning and Compensation Act 1991 specifies the date from which interest is payable on compensation arising under the statutory provisions mentioned, which do not themselves provide for the payment of interest.

items.title: The Railways (Interoperability) (High-Speed) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations implement in relation to the whole of the United Kingdom, Council Directive 96/48/EC of 23rd July 1996 on the interoperability of the trans-European high-speed rail system (O.J. No. L235, 17.9.97, p.6 as corrected by O.J. 1262, 16.10.96. p.18). The purpose of that Directive is to bring about the inter-working (interoperability) of rolling stock on the trans-European high-speed rail system and, in particular to introduce common standards of construction across the EC. The Regulations apply to the United Kingdom lines on the high-speed trans-European rail system (“the rail network schemes”).

items.title: The Health Act 1999 (Commencement No. 12) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st April 2002 section 60(3) of the Health Act 1999 (partially) and repeals in Schedule 5 to the Act (partially). The effect is that certain provisions in the Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors Act 1997 and the Professions Supplementary to Medicine Act 1960 are repealed in consequence of the coming into force of the Nursing and Midwifery Order 2001 (S.I. 2002/253) and the Health Professions Order 2001 (S.I. 2002/254). Other provisions in the 1997 and 1960 Acts were repealed by virtue of S.I. 2001/1985.

items.title: The A6 Trunk Road (Derby to Stockport) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A6 Trunk Road (Derby to Stockport) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors (Amendment) Rules Approval Order 2002
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order, made by the United Kingdom Central Council for Nursing, Midwifery and Health Visiting, further amend the Rules approved by the Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors Rules Approval Order 1983 (S.I. 1983/873) to provide for the payment of the registration fee by annual amounts; for a shortened period of training for students with approved degrees; and for the removal of the requirement that indexes of students be kept.

items.title: The Social Security Fraud Act 2001 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2002
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into force of section 3 of the Social Security Fraud Act 2001 (which makes provision in relation to a code of practice about the use of information powers). Section 3 is commenced on 28th January 2002.

items.title: The Health and Social Care Act 2001 (Commencement No. 8) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This order amends the Health and Social Care Act 2001 (Commencement No. 8) Order 2002 (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The Education (Governors' Annual Reports) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Governor’s Annual Reports) (England) Regulations 1999 (“the 1999 Regulations”) by substituting a new Schedule setting out the information to be contained in governors' annual reports.

items.title: The Education (School Information) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (School Information) (England) Regulations 1998 which require local education authorities in England to publish for each school year a composite prospectus of admission information for schools in their area.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Transport) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st June 2002, amends Group 8 of Schedule 8 to the Value Added Tax Act 1994 (c. 23).

items.title: The Radioactive Substances (Natural Gas) Exemption Order 2002
items.description: This Order provides exemptions from the registration and authorisation requirements of the Radioactive Substances Act 1993 in relation to natural gas and natural gas products which contain limited amounts of radioactivity.

items.title: The A45 Trunk Road (Weedon Road, Upton To The M45/A45 Roundabout, Dunchurch) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A45 Trunk Road (Weedon Road, Upton To The M45/A45 Roundabout, Dunchurch) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO.
