It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A38 Trunk Road (Worcestershire/Gloucestershire) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A38 Trunk Road (Worcestershire/Gloucestershire) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Foot-and-Mouth Disease (Marking of Meat, Meat Preparations and Meat Products) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Meat Products (Hygiene) Regulations 1994, the Fresh Meat (Hygiene and Inspection) Regulations 1995 and the Minced Meat and Meat Preparations (Hygiene) Regulations 1995. They implement Commission Decision 2002/49/EC amending for the second time Decision 2001/304/EC on marking and use of certain animal products in relation to Decision 2001/172/EC concerning certain protection measures with regard to foot-and-mouth disease in the United Kingdom (OJ No. L21, 24.1.2001, p. 30).

items.title: The A452 Trunk Road (B5011 Ogley Road Junction To The A4041 Queslett Road East/B4138 Thornhill Road Roundabout) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A452 Trunk Road (B5011 Ogley Road Junction To The A4041 Queslett Road East/B4138 Thornhill Road Roundabout) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A523 Trunk Road (Calton Moor to Cheshire Border) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A523 Trunk Road (Calton Moor to Cheshire Border) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A516 Trunk Road (West of Derby) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A516 Trunk Road (West of Derby) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.description: This version of the The A523 Trunk Road (County of Cheshire) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A523 Trunk Road (Metropolitan Borough of Stockport) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A523 Trunk Road (Metropolitan Borough of Stockport) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A61 Trunk Road (Alfreton to Sheffield) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A61 Trunk Road (Alfreton to Sheffield) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A52 Trunk Road (Derby to Calton Moor) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A52 Trunk Road (Derby to Calton Moor) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Rail Vehicle Accessibility (Middleton Railway Drewry Car) Exemption Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The Rail Vehicle Accessibility (Middleton Railway Drewry Car) Exemption Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Import and Export Restrictions (Foot-And-Mouth Disease) (No. 3) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to England, implement Commission Decision 2002/48/EC amending for the seventh time Decision 2001/740/EC concerning certain protection measures with regard to foot-and-mouth disease in the United Kingdom (OJ No. L21, 24.1.2002, p. 29). They revoke and replace the Import and Export Restrictions (Foot-and-Mouth Disease) (No. 2) Regulations 2002 (S. I. 2002/76).

items.title: The Child Support (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations provide for the amendment of regulations relating to child support. Regulation 1 makes provision for citation, commencement and interpretation.

items.title: The A57 Trunk Road (M1 to A1) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A57 Trunk Road (M1 to A1) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A606 Trunk Road (A52 to A46) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A606 Trunk Road (A52 to A46) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.description: This version of the The A614 Trunk Road (Leapool to Ollerton and Blyth to Bawtry) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A15 Trunk Road (North of Lincoln to North Lincolnshire Border) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A15 Trunk Road (North of Lincoln to North Lincolnshire Border) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A15 Trunk Road (South of M180) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A15 Trunk Road (South of M180) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A16 Trunk Road (Stamford to North East Lincolnshire) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A16 Trunk Road (Stamford to North East Lincolnshire) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A17 Trunk Road (Newark-on-Trent to Kings Lynn) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A17 Trunk Road (Newark-on-Trent to Kings Lynn) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A46 Trunk Road (North of Lincoln) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A46 Trunk Road (North of Lincoln) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A34 Trunk Road (Newtown, Great Wyrley, Staffordshire To The Southern Boundary Of The A34/A500 Queensway Roundabout, Staffordshire) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A34 Trunk Road (Newtown, Great Wyrley, Staffordshire To The Southern Boundary Of The A34/A500 Queensway Roundabout, Staffordshire) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A5011 Trunk Road (Linley Road) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A5011 Trunk Road (Linley Road) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A449 Trunk Road (A5 Gailey Roundabout to the A34 Queensway, Stafford) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A449 Trunk Road (A5 Gailey Roundabout to the A34 Queensway, Stafford) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security Fraud Act 2001 (Commencement No. 5) Order 2002
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into force of further provisions of the Social Security Fraud Act 2001 (“the Fraud Act”) on 30th April 2002:

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Major Infrastructure Project Inquiries Procedure) (England) Rules 2002
items.description: These Rules regulate the procedure to be followed in connection with local inquiries relating to major infrastructure projects in England held by the Secretary of State before he determines applications referred to him, or appeals made to him, in relation to planning permission, listed building consent and consent for the demolition of unlisted buildings in conservation areas (known as “conservation area consent”) required in respect of a major infrastructure project (defined in rule 2 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Products of Animal Origin (Third Country Imports) (England) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement for England Council Directive 97/78/EC laying down the principles governing the organisation of veterinary checks on products entering the Community from third countries (OJ No. L24, 30.1.98, p.9). The Directive applies to products of animal origin—meat, fish (including shellfish), milk and products made from these, together with egg products and a large number of animal by-products, including casings, skins, bones and blood. When read with Annex I to the Agreement on the European Economic Area (OJ No. L1, 3.1.94, p.3, as amended) the Directive requires that products of animal origin which enter the European Economic Area must undergo specified veterinary checks at border inspection posts to ensure that they comply with requirements laid down by Community legislation for the protection of animal and public health.

items.title: The Cornwall Healthcare National Health Service Trust Change of Name and (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Cornwall Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1993, which established the Cornwall Healthcare National Health Service Trust, and changes the name of that trust.

items.title: The Lincolnshire South West Primary Care Trust Change of Name Order 2002
items.description: This Order changes the name of the Lincolnshire South West Primary Care Trust to the Lincolnshire South West Teaching Primary Care Trust.

items.title: The Insolvency Act 1986 (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations are to make amendments to the Insolvency Act 1986 (c. 45) (“the Act”) as a result of the adoption by the Council of the European Union of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1346/2000 of 29th May 2000 on insolvency proceedings Official Journal No. L160, 30.06.00. p.1 (“the EC Regulation”) which comes into force on 31st May 2002.

items.title: The Immigration (Swiss Free Movement of Persons) (No. 3) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations give effect to the Agreement between the European Community and its Member States, of the one part, and the Swiss Confederation, of the other part, on the Free Movement of Persons, signed at Brussels on 21st June 1999 (Cm 4904).

items.title: The Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Administration Orders Relating to Insurers) Order 2002
items.description: This Order applies, in relation to insurers, Part II of the Insolvency Act 1986 with various modifications set out in the Schedule. Article 2 provides that the definition of “insurer” for these purposes is to be that contained in article 2 of the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Insolvency) (Definition of “Insurer”) Order 2001 (S.I. 2001/2634). Article 3 enables administration orders to be made in relation to such insurers. Article 5 provides that insolvency rules relating to mutual credit and set-off do not apply to sums due from an insurer to another party where, at the time the sums became due, an application for an administration order had been made in relation to the insurer.

items.title: The Blackpool, Fylde and Wyre Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the Blackpool, Wyre and Fylde Community Health Services National Health Service Trust and the Blackpool Victoria Hospital National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Blackpool, Fylde and Wyre Hospitals National Health Service Trust as an NHS trust, provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The Cheshire and Wirral Partnership National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the Wirral and West Cheshire Community National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Cheshire and Wirral Partnership NHS Trust National Health Service Trust as an NHS trust, provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 (article 2).

items.title: The Care Standards Act 2000 (Commencement No. 12) (England) Order 2002
items.description: This order provides for the coming into force on 25th March 2002 of the following provisions of the Care Standards Act 2000 (C. 14)—

items.title: The Postal Services Act 2000 (Determination of Turnover for Penalties) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends article 2(2) of the Postal Services Act (Determination of Turnover for Penalties) Order 2001 (“the principal order”).

items.title: The National Care Standards Commission (Children’s Rights Director) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations are made under paragraph 10(2) of Schedule 1 to the Care Standards Act 2000 and prescribe the functions of the children’s rights director who is to be appointed by, and to be a member of the staff of, the National Care Standards Commission.

items.title: The Manchester Mental Health and Social Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Manchester Mental Health and Social Care Trust as an NHS trust designated as a Care Trust, as provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 and section 45 of the Health and Social Care Act 2001 (article 2). The Order provides for the functions of the trust (article 3) and the number of executive and non-executive directors (article 4). It specifies the operational date (the date on which the trust assumes all its functions) as the day on which the trust is established, and the accounting date of the trust (article 5). This Order does not impose a charge on businesses.

items.title: The British Overseas Territories Act 2002 (Commencement) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings into force those provisions of the British Overseas Territories Act 2002 which are not already in force.

items.title: The TSE (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the TSE (England) Regulations 2002, S.I. 2002/843, to correct errors in those Regulations. Copies of these amending Regulations are being issued free of charge to all known purchasers of S.I. 2002/843 by the TSE Directorate of the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, 1A Page Street, London SW1P 4PQ.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area And Special Parking Area) (City Of Southampton) Order 2002
items.description: This Order designates the City of Southampton, excluding the M27 motorway and the M271 motorway, together with all its on and off slip roads, from its junction with the Redbridge Roundabout for the whole of its entire length in the City of Southampton, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The General Chiropractic Council (Election of Members and Chairman of Council) Rules Order 2002
items.description: This Order, which is made under the Chiropractors Act 1994, approves Rules made by the General Chiropractic Council (“the Council”) which provide for the procedure both for the election of registered chiropractors as members of the Council for the period following the end of the transitional period mentioned in paragraphs 43 and 44 of Schedule 1 to the Act and for the election of a Chairman of the Council.

items.title: The Water Industry (Scotland) Act 2002 (Directions in the Interests of National Security) Order 2002
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential on the Water Industry (Scotland) Act 2002 which, amongst other things, established Scottish Water and repealed section 117 of the Local Government etc. (Scotland) Act 1994 (“the 1994 Act”). Section 117 of the 1994 Act enabled the Secretary of State to give directions to the new water and sewerage authorities (now replaced by Scottish Water) in the interests of national security or for mitigating the effects of any civil emergency. This provision was amended (in part) by article 3 of and Schedule 2 to the Scotland Act 1998 (Transfer of Functions to the Scottish Ministers etc) Order 1999 (“the 1999 Order”), so that the Scottish Ministers could also give directions, after consultation with the Secretary of State. The purpose of this Order is to confer on the Secretary of State and the Scottish Ministers the power to give directions to Scottish Water in the interests of national security or for mitigating the effects of any civil emergency.

items.title: The Beer and Excise Warehousing (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st June 2002, amend the Beer Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/1228) and the Excise Warehousing (Etc.) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/809).

items.title: The Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 (Codes of Practice) (Visual Recording of Interviews) Order 2002
items.description: This Order appoints 7th May 2002 as the date on which the code of practice for the visual recording for police interviews under section 60A(1) of the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 will come into operation.

items.title: The Pressure Equipment (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Pressure Equipment Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/2001) (“the 1999 Regulations”) by providing that pressure equipment that is placed on the market after it has been used otherwise than in the course of business at all times since its manufacture or import shall not be subject to the requirements of the 1999 Regulations relating to placing on the market of such equipment (regulation 2(1)). They also increase the penalty for certain offences created by the 1999 Regulations (regulation 2(2)). Regulation 2(3) corrects a cross-reference in the 1999 Regulations.

items.title: The Firearms (Amendment) Act 1988 (Firearms Consultative Committee) Order 2002
items.description: This Order continues in existence the Firearms Consultative Committee established under section 22 of the Firearms (Amendment) Act 1988 for a further period of two years from 1st February 2002. That Committee has been kept in existence by the Orders under section 22(8) of that Act revoked by article 3 of this Order.

items.title: The Coast Protection (Notices) (England) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations replace the Coast Protection (Notices) Regulations 1950 in England. They prescribe a form of notice for coast protection authorities to give of proposed coast protection work under section 5 of the Coast Protection Act 1949. They also set out the requirements for service of such notices on statutory bodies and other interested parties and for the service of notices of objection.

items.title: The Anti-terrorism, Crime and Security Act 2001 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings Part 7 of the Anti-terrorism, Crime and Security Act 2001 (security of pathogens and toxins) into force on 31st May 2002.

items.title: The Milk Marketing Board (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: In accordance with section 14(5) of the Agriculture Act 1993 (“the Act”), this Order dissolves the Milk Marketing Board (“the Board”). The Order comes into force on 31st January 2002.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Special Provisions) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st June 2002, amends the Value Added Tax (Special Provisions) Order 1995 (S.I. 1995/1268).

items.title: The Security of Pathogens and Toxins (Exceptions to Dangerous Substances) Regulations 2002
items.description: Part 7 of the Anti-terrorism, Crime and Security Act 2001 places duties on the occupiers of premises at which dangerous substances are kept. In that Part, a “dangerous substance” means anything which consists of or includes a substance for the time being mentioned in Schedule 5 to that Act (section 58(4)(a)) or anything which is infected with or otherwise carries any such substance (section 58(4)(b)). However, under section 58(5) to the Act something which otherwise falls within that definition is not to be regarded as a dangerous substance if it satisfies conditions prescribed in regulations or is kept or used in circumstances so prescribed. These Regulations exercise those powers.

items.title: The Police Authorities (Selection Panel) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Authorities (Selection Panel) Regulations 1994.

items.title: The Rotherham Priority Health Services National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves on 1st April 2002:

items.title: The Barnsley Community and Priority Services National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves on 1st April 2002:

items.title: The Doncaster Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order further amends the Doncaster Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1991, which established the Doncaster Healthcare National Health Service Trust, which changed its name to the Doncaster and South Humber Healthcare National Health Service Trust by the Doncaster Healthcare National Health Service Trust Change of Name and (Establishment) Amendment Order 1999.

items.title: The Southern Derbyshire Mental Health National Health Service Trust Change of Name and (Establishment) Amendment Order and the Community Health Care Service (North Derbyshire) National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Southern Derbyshire Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1992, which established the Southern Derbyshire Mental Health National Health Service Trust, and changes the name of that trust.

items.title: The Sheffield Children’s Hospital National Health Service Trust Change of Name and (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Sheffield Children’s Hospital National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1991, which established the Sheffield Children’s National Health Service Trust, and changes the name of that trust.

items.title: The Regulation of Investigatory Powers (Prescription of Offices, Ranks and Positions) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Regulation of Investigatory Powers (Prescription of Offices, Ranks and Positions) Order 2000 by changing the offices, ranks and position proscribed in respect of the National Criminal Intelligence Service (“NCIS”). The Order provides that for NCIS the persons designated for the purposes of granting an authorisation under section 28 and section 29 of the Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act 2000 are, in addition to a Superintendent, any individual holding a position at Grade M4, or any individual on secondment to NCIS who holds an office, rank or position which is designated for this purpose in the public authority from where he is seconded. The Order similarly amends the offices, ranks and positions in NCIS prescribed for urgent cases.

items.title: The Plant Health (Phytophthora ramorum) (England) Order 2002
items.description: This Order implements measures to prevent the introduction and spread of the pest Phytophthora ramorum, a fungus which is not established in the United Kingdom. It regulates the importation and movement into and within England of plant material identified as susceptible to the pest, believed to cause Sudden Oak Death syndrome in certain species of oak in the USA, and harm to Rhododendron, Viburnum and other plants.

items.title: The Insolvency (Amendment) Rules 2002
items.description: These Rules amend the Insolvency Rules 1986 (S.I. 1986/1925) (“the 1986 Rules”) in the light of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1346/2000 of 29th May 2000 on insolvency proceedings, OJ No. L160, 30.06.00. p.1 (the “EC Regulation”) which comes into force on 31st May 2002.

items.title: The Insolvent Partnerships (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Insolvent Partnerships Order 1994 (S.I. 1994/2421) (“the 1994 Order”) in the light of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1346/2000 of 29th May 2000 on insolvency proceedings (“the EC Regulation”) which comes into force on 31st May 2002.

items.title: The Administration of Insolvent Estates of Deceased Persons (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Administration of Insolvent Estates of Deceased Persons Order 1986 (S.I. 1986/1999) (“the 1986 Order”) in the light of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1346/2000 of 29th May 2000 on insolvency proceedings (“the EC Regulation”) which comes into force on 31st May 2002.

items.title: The Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Financial Promotion and Miscellaneous Amendments) Order 2002
items.description: This Order makes a number of miscellaneous amendments to orders made under the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (“the Act”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Compensation for Premature Retirement) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with minor amendments, the provisions of the National Health Service (Compensation for Premature Retirement) Regulations 1981. They provide for the payment of compensation to or in respect of a person who was employed in the National Health Service in England and Wales and has prematurely retired from that employment by reason of redundancy or in the interests of the efficiency of the service.

items.title: The Health and Social Care Act 2001 (Commencement No. 9) Order 2002
items.description: This Order is the ninth Commencement Order under the Health and Social Care Act 2001 (“the Act”).

items.title: The South West Yorkshire Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the Wakefield and Pontefract Community National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the South West Yorkshire Mental Health National Health Service Trust as an NHS trust, provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The A596 Trunk Road (Calva Brow Junction, Workington) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A596 Trunk Road (Calva Brow Junction, Workington) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Student Support) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Student Support) Regulations 2002 (“the Regulations”) which came into force on 25th February 2002.

items.title: The Bradford District Care Trust (Establishment) and the Bradford Community Health National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Bradford District Care Trust as an NHS trust designated as a Care Trust, as provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 and section 45 of the Health and Social Care Act 2001.

items.title: The National Health Service Trusts (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves on 1st April 2002 the National Health Services Trusts established by the National Health Service Trust Orders which are listed in the Schedule. Those Orders are accordingly revoked.

items.title: The South of Tyne and Wearside Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the Priority Healthcare Wearside National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the South of Tyne and Wearside Mental Health National Health Service Trust as an NHS trust, provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The Primary Care Trusts (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves a number of Primary Care Trusts whose areas have become parts of other Primary Care Trusts with effect from 1st April 2002. It also revokes the relevant establishment orders.

items.title: The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) Amendment (No. 3) (England) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) Regulations 1997 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for payments to be made by means of a voucher system in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with sight tests and the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The Greater Manchester (Light Rapid Transit System) (Trafford Depot) Order 2002
items.description: This Order authorises Greater Manchester Passenger Transport Executive to construct a servicing and maintenance depot for its Metrolink system at a site in and adjoining Elsinore Road in the borough of Trafford.

items.title: The Animal Gatherings (Interim Measures) (England) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order applies only to England. It amends the Animal Gatherings (Interim Measures) (England) Order 2002 (S.I. 2002/202) (the principal Order), which for a temporary period until 30 November 2002 disapplies and replaces the Markets, Sales and Lairs Order 1925 (S.I. 1925/1349) (as amended), and prohibits the use of premises for animal gatherings unless there is a licence permitting that activity and unless the provisions which are set out in its Schedule are adhered to.

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) (Amendment) (England and Wales) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st August 2002, amend the Education (Student Loans) Regulations 1998 governing loans made under the Education (Student Loans) Act 1990, which are mortgage style repayment loans made, for the most part, to students who began their courses before 1st August 1998.

items.title: The Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st September 2002, supersede the Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 2001 as amended (“the 2001 Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (QCA Levy) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These regulations make an amendment to regulation 5 of the Education (QCA Levy) Regulations 2002, which provide for the imposition of a levy on persons awarding National Vocational Qualifications which are accredited by the Qualifications and Curriculum Authority. The amendment corrects a drafting error.

items.title: The Tax Credits (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 3) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1973) and the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/2887).

items.title: The Tax Credits (Prescribed Period of Awards) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations provide that where an award of working families tax credit or disabled person’s tax credit becomes payable on or after 4th June 2002, the period for which that award shall be payable is the period that begins on the date on which it becomes payable and ends on 7th April 2003, rather than the period of 26 weeks specified in sections 128(3) and 129(6) of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992.

items.title: The Wiltshire and Swindon Health Care National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves on 1st April 2002:

items.title: The National Health Service Trusts (Originating Capital) Order 2002
items.description: This Order determines the amount of the originating capital provided for in Section 9 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 of NHS Trusts established under that Act in the year beginning 1 April 2001.

items.title: The West Kent National Health Service and Social Care Trust (Establishment) and the Thames Gateway National Health Service Trust and Invicta Community Care National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the West Kent National Health Service and Social Care Trust as an NHS trust, provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The Bournewood Community and Mental Health National Health Service Trust Change of Name Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Bournewood Community and Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1994, which established the Bournewood Community and Mental Health National Health Service Trust, and changes the name of that trust.

items.title: The Tax Credits (Prescribed Period of Awards) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations provide that where an award of working families' tax credit or disabled person’s tax credit becomes payable on or after 4th June 2002, the period for which that award shall be payable is the period that begins on the date on which it becomes payable and ends on 7th April 2003, rather than the period of 26 weeks specified in sections 127(3) and 128(6) of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992.

items.title: The Tax Credits (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 2) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (S.R. 1987 No. 463) and the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (S.R. 1992 No. 78).

items.title: The Mid Yorkshire Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the Pinderfields and Pontefract Hospitals National Health Service Trust and the Dewsbury Health Care National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Mid Yorkshire Hospitals National Health Service Trust as an NHS trust, provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The Northallerton Health Services National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves on 1st April 2002:

items.title: The Judicial Pensions (Pensions Appeal Tribunals) Order 2002
items.description: This Order adds the office of Deputy President of the Pensions Appeal Tribunals to the list of qualifying judicial offices in Schedule 1 to the Judicial Pensions and Retirement Act 1993.

items.title: The Disease Control (Interim Measures) (England) (Amendment No. 2) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Disease Control (Interim Measures) (England) Order 2002 (the “principal Order”) (S.I. 2002/242) (as amended by S.I. 2002/907).

items.title: The Sheep and Goat Identification and Movement (Interim Measures) (England) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 2002
items.description: This Order applies only to England. It amends the Sheep and Goats Identification and Movement (Interim Measures) (England) Order 2002 (S.I. 2002/240) (the principal Order), which for a temporary period until 30th November 2002, imposes requirements relating to the identification, registration and movement of sheep and goats.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Hampshire) (District of Hart) Order 2002
items.description: This Order designates whole of the District of Hart, with the exception of the M3 motorway, together with all its on and off slip roads, for its entire length in the District of Hart, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Hampshire) (Borough of Rushmoor) Order 2002
items.description: This Order applies to the whole of the Borough of Rushmoor with the exception of the M3 motorway, together with all its on and off slip roads, for its entire length within the Borough of Rushmoor. The Order designates the Borough of Rushmoor as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (City of Liverpool) Order 2002
items.description: This Order designates the City of Liverpool, with the exception of the M62 motorway, including its on and off slip roads, for its entire length in the City, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Offshore Chemicals Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 2 of the Pollution Prevention and Control Act 1999. They establish a regime for the purpose of implementing the United Kingdom’s obligations under the Convention for the Protection of the Marine Environment of the North-East Atlantic (OSPAR) Decision (2000/2) on a Harmonised Mandatory Control System for the Use and Reduction of the Discharge of Offshore Chemicals in relation to offshore activities (defined in regulation 2).

items.title: The Local Government Act 2000 (Commencement No. 3) (Wales) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings into force subsection (2) of section 93 of the Local Government Act 2000 which enables grants to be made to local authorities in Wales for welfare services, and subsections (3) to (12) of that section insofar as they apply to local authorities in Wales.

items.title: The Worcestershire Community and Mental Health National Health Service Trust Change of Name and (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Worcestershire Community and Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1999, which established the Worcestershire Community and Mental Health National Health Service Trust, and changes the name of that trust.

items.title: The Brent, Kensington, Chelsea and Westminster Mental Health National Health Service Trust Change of Name and (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Brent, Kensington, Chelsea and Westminster Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1998, which established the Brent, Kensington, Chelsea and Westminster Mental Health National Health Service Trust, and changes the name of that trust.

items.title: The West Sussex Health and Social Care National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the Worthing Priority National Health Service Trust and Sussex Weald and Downs National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the West Sussex Health and Social Care National Health Service Trust as an NHS trust, provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The Brighton and Sussex University Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the Mid Sussex National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Brighton and Sussex University Hospitals National Health Service Trust as an NHS trust, provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The Sandwell and West Birmingham Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the City Hospital National Health Service Trust and Sandwell Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Sandwell and West Birmingham Hospitals National Health Service Trust as an NHS trust, provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The Scarborough, Whitby and Ryedale Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Scarborough, Whitby and Ryedale Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The Police (Retention and Disposal of Items Seized) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations revoke in relation to England and Wales the Police (Retention and Disposal of Items seized under section 60 of the Criminal Justice and Public Order Act 1994) Regulations 1999 in consequence of the amendment of that section and the addition of section 60AA to the 1994 Act (powers to require removal of disguises) by the Anti-terrorism, Crime and Security Act 2001. The amendments to the 1994 Act made by the 2001 Act do not extend to Scotland. These regulations make provision for the retention, safe-keeping, disposal and destruction of items seized under section 60 and 60AA in England and Wales. They have the same effect as the 1999 Regulations which will continue to apply in Scotland.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (AT&T Global Network Services (UK) B.V.) Order 2002
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to AT&T Global Network Services (UK) B.V. a licence on 15 March 2002 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 16th May 2002. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, AT&T Global Network Services (UK) B.V. will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 14th June 2002.

items.title: The Education (Listed Bodies) (England) Order 2002
items.description: This Order lists the name of each body which is not a recognised body within section 214(2)(a) or (b) of the Education Reform Act 1988 but which either—

items.title: The Tax Credits (Decisions and Appeals) (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security and Child Support (Decisions and Appeals) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (S.R. 1999 No. 162) (“the principal Regulations”) in relation to working families' tax credit and disabled person’s tax credit.

items.title: The Social Security and Child Support (Decisions and Appeals) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security and Child Support (Decisions and Appeals) Regulations 1999. In particular—

items.title: The Middlesbrough Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Middlesbrough Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes (Disclosure of Information) Amendment Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Personal Pension Schemes (Disclosure of Information) Regulations 1987 (“the 1987 Regulations”), the Occupational Pension Schemes (Disclosure of Information) Regulations 1996 (“the 1996 Regulations”) and the Stakeholder Pension Schemes Regulations 2000 (“the 2000 Regulations”) following the amendment of section 113 of the Pension Schemes Act 1993 by section 52 of the Child Support, Pensions and Social Security Act 2000.

items.title: The East Lancashire (Heywood Extension) Light Railway Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The East Lancashire (Heywood Extension) Light Railway Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations correct an omission from the National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) Amendment Regulations 2002 (S.I. 2002/548) by increasing the charge payable for a single item of elastic hosiery under regulation 4(1)(a) of the National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) Regulations 2000.

items.title: The Sunderland Teaching Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Sunderland Teaching Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The North Stoke Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2002
items.description: This Order further amends the establishment order of North Stoke Primary Care Trust, a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977 by making changes to the area in which the North Stoke Primary Care Trust is to undertake its functions.

items.title: The South Stoke Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2002
items.description: This Order further amends the establishment order of the South Stoke Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977 by making changes to the area in which the trust is to undertake its functions.

items.title: The A39 Trunk Road (Devon/Cornwall County Boundary to Indian Queens Cornwall) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A39 Trunk Road (Devon/Cornwall County Boundary to Indian Queens Cornwall) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Secretaries of State for Education and Skills and for Work and Pensions Order 2002
items.description: This Order in Council, made under the Ministers of the Crown Act 1975, constitutes the Secretary of State for Education and Skills and the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions as corporations sole (articles 3 and 4). Those articles also provide for the authentication of the corporate seals, the making of instruments by those Secretaries of State and related matters.

items.title: The European Convention on Cinematographic Co-production (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: The European Convention on Cinematographic Co-production Order 1994 (“the Principal Order”) provides that films made in accordance with the European Convention on Cinematographic Co-production (Cm 2495) are to be treated as British films for the purposes of Schedule 1 to the Films Act 1985. For the Convention to apply where there are only two co-producers one must be established in the United Kingdom and the other in one of the countries set out in the Schedule to the Principal Order. For the Convention to apply where there are three or more co-producers one must be established in the United Kingdom and at least two others in different countries set out in the Schedule to the Principal Order.

items.title: The Dentists Act 1984 (Dental Auxiliaries) Order 2002
items.description: Section 46(1)(b) of the Dentists Act 1984 stipulates that the filling of teeth and the extraction of deciduous teeth can only be undertaken by dental auxiliaries in the course of the provision of national health services. This order disapplies this provision in relation to dental therapists.

items.title: The Tax Credits (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations 1991 and the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987 with effect for award periods of working families' tax credit and disabled person’s tax credit commencing on or after 29th January 2002.

items.title: The Langbaurgh Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Langbaurgh Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The Brinsbury College (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 1st August 2002 the further education corporation established to conduct Brinsbury College. It provides for the transfer of its property, rights and liabilities to Chichester College of Arts, Science and Technology and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The Primary Care Trusts (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order removes from all those PCT establishment orders listed in the Schedule the article (article 4) which sets out their membership. It also makes consequential amendments to the definitions in article 1. In future, PCTs will be free to set their own membership within the constraints of the national framework as set out in the Primary Care Trust (Membership, Procedure and Administration Arrangements) Regulations 2000 (S.I. 2000/89).

items.title: The Social Security (Attendance Allowance and Disability Living Allowance) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations 1991 (“the Attendance Allowance Regulations”) and the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations 1991 (“the Disability Living Allowance Regulations”).

items.title: The Gaming Clubs (Bankers' Games) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: Section 13(1) of the Gaming Act 1968 prohibits the playing of bankers' games and games of unequal chance on premises licensed under that Act. Section 13(2), however, authorises the making of regulations to provide that this prohibition shall not have effect in relation to games specified in the Regulations, if so played as to comply with the Regulations.

items.title: The International Development Act 2002 (Commencement) Order 2002
items.description: By this Order the Secretary of State for International Development brings into force the International Development Act 2002 (which received Royal Assent on 26th February 2002) on 17th June 2002.

items.title: The Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Consequential Amendments) (Taxes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order makes amendments to tax legislation in consequence of the provisions of the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (c. 8) (“the FSMA”).

items.title: The Sedgefield Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Sedgefield Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provisions for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The Lotteries (Variation of Monetary Limits) Order 2002
items.description: This Order varies certain of the monetary limits specified in section 11 of the Lotteries and Amusements Act 1976 (c. 32) which apply to societies' lotteries and local lotteries. The maximum price of a ticket or chance in such a lottery is increased from £1 to £2, the maximum value of tickets or chances which may be sold in one such lottery is increased from £1,000,000 to £2,000,000, and the maximum value of tickets or chances sold in all such lotteries held in any one year and promoted on behalf of the same society or by the same local authority is increased from £5,000,000 to £10,000,000.

items.title: The Income Support (General) and Jobseeker’s Allowance Amendment Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967) and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207) so as to amend the definition of “self-employment route” to include assistance in pursuing self-employed earner’s employment whilst participating in the New Deal for Lone Parents. This brings the calculation of income of such participants within the scope of the special rules contained in Chapter IVA of Part V of the 1987 Regulations and in Chapter IVA of Part VIII of the 1996 Regulations.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Community Authorisations) (Modification of the Road Traffic (Foreign Vehicles) Act 1972) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations modify the Road Traffic (Foreign Vehicles) Act 1972 (“the 1972 Act”) in order to provide an express power to examiners to prohibit goods vehicles which do not conform with the requirements of the Goods Vehicles (Community Authorisations) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/3077) (“the 1992 Regulations”). The 1992 Regulations give effect in the United Kingdom to Council Regulation (EEC) No. 881/92 of 26 March 1992 (O.J. No. L95, 9.4.1992, p.1) (“the EC Regulation”).

items.title: The Tax Credit (New Category of Child Care Provider) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations make a scheme for establishing a new category of persons whose charges for providing child care outside the United Kingdom are to be taken into account for the purposes of determining working families' tax credit or disabled person’s tax credit. The persons whose charges can be taken into account for these purposes must be approved by an organisation that has been accredited by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Free Zone (Port of Tilbury) Designation Order 2002
items.description: This Order replaces the Free Zone (Port of Tilbury) Designation Order 1992 (S.I. 1991 No. 1282) as amended by the Free Zone (Port of Tilbury) Designation (Variation) Order 1994 (S.I. 1994 No. 2216). With effect from 2nd June 2002, it designates an area of 766.9 acres at the Port of Tilbury, Essex, as a Free Zone. It appoints the responsible authority and imposes obligations upon that authority relating to the operation of the zone.

items.title: The Deregulation (Correction of Birth and Death Entries in Registers or Other Records) Order 2002
items.description: This Order is made under section 1 of the Deregulation and Contracting Out Act 1994. It introduces an alternative procedure for certain corrections in registers of live-births, still-births or deaths under section 29(3) of the Births and Deaths Registration Act 1953 and in registers or other records under section 3(3) of the Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Special Provisions) Act 1957. The procedure applies where in a birth or death entry a person is wrongly shown as the father of the person to whose birth or death the entry relates.

items.title: The Easington Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Easington Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The North Kirklees Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the North Kirklees Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) (Amendment) (No. 2) (England and Wales) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st August 2002, amend the Education (Student Loans) Regulations 1998 governing loans made under the Education (Student Loans) Act 1990, which are mortgage style repayment loans made, for the most part, to students who began their courses before 1st August 1998.

items.title: The Education (Teachers' Qualifications and Health Standards) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These regulations amend the Education (Teachers' Qualifications and Health Standards) (England) Regulations (S.I. 1999/2166).

items.title: The Race Relations Act 1976 (General Statutory Duty: Code of Practice) Order 2002
items.description: This Order appoints 31st May 2002 as the day on which the code of practice containing the Commission for Racial Equality’s guidance in relation to the performance of the duties under section 71(1) and (2) of the Race Relations Act 1976 (as amended by the Race Relations (Amendment) Act 2000) is to come into effect. The duty under section 71(1), which applies to certain specified public authorities, is a general duty to have due regard, in the exercise of their functions, to the need to eliminate unlawful racial discrimination and to promote equality of opportunity and good relations between persons of different racial groups. The duties under section 71(2) are duties imposed on certain of those authorities for the purpose of ensuring the better performance of the general duty.

items.title: The Protection of Children Act 1999 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2002
items.description: This Order is the third commencement order made under the Protection of Children Act 1999. It brings into force on 12th March 2002 section 8 of the Act. Section 8 amends sections 113 and 115 of the Police Act 1997 (c. 50), which relate to criminal record certificates that may be issued under that Act.

items.title: The Leicestershire and Rutland Healthcare National Health Service Trust Change of Name and (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Leicestershire and Rutland Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1998 (“the Establishment Order”) which established the Leicestershire and Rutland Healthcare National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The Health Service (Control of Patient Information) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the processing of patient information, including confidential patient information.

items.title: The Calderdale Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Calderdale Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: Commission Areas (West Mercia) Order 2002
items.description: This Order provides for the replacement of the two commission areas of Hereford and Worcester and Shropshire by a single commission area to be know as the West Mercia commission area, and comes into force on 21st June 2002.

items.title: The Derwentside Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Derwentside Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The Regulatory Reform (Carer’s Allowance) Order 2002
items.description: This Order is made under section 1 of the Regulatory Reform Act 2001. It makes a number of reforms to section 70 of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (“the Act”), which provides for an allowance (“invalid care allowance”) for those caring for severely disabled people.

items.title: The Disability Discrimination (Educational Institutions) (Alteration of Leasehold Premises) Regulations 2002
items.description: These regulations are made under paragraph 13 of Schedule 6 of the Disability Discrimination Act 1995 and contain provisions in respect of consents required under the terms of leases and sub-leases occupied by educational institutions whose governing bodies are subject to the duty of reasonable adjustment set out in section 28T of that Act.

items.title: The Disability Discrimination (Designation of Educational Institutions) Order 2002
items.description: This Order designates those institutions in England, Wales and Scotland which, together with others, are educational institutions for the purposes of Part IV of the Disability Discrimination Act and identifies the body responsible for each. The effect of designation is to place the responsible body under a duty to comply with the provisions of Part IV.

items.title: The Gateshead Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Gateshead Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The Plant Protection Products (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to England and Wales, further amend the Plant Protection Products Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/887) which implement in Great Britain Council Directive 91/414/EEC concerning the placing of plant protection products on the market (OJ No. L230, 19.8.91 p.1).

items.title: The Home-Grown Cereals Authority (Rate of Levy) Order 2002
items.description: For the purposes of financing the Home-Grown Cereals Authority’s non-trading functions under Part I of the Cereals Marketing Act 1965 for the year beginning on 1st July 2002, this Order specifies the rates of dealer levy, grower levy and processor levies which appear to the Ministers and the National Assembly for Wales to be sufficient to meet the amount apportioned to certain cereals grown in the United Kingdom, namely, wheat (including durum wheat), barley, oats, rye, maize, triticale, or any two or more such cereals grown as one crop, and the rate of levy which appears to them to be sufficient to meet the amount apportioned to certain oilseeds grown in the United Kingdom, namely rapeseed, linseed, soyabean, sunflowerseed or any two or more of such oilseeds grown as one crop.

items.title: The Durham Dales Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Durham Dales Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The Hydrocarbon Oil (Industrial Reliefs) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st July 2002 for the purposes of regulations 5 and 6 (applications for, and certificates of, approval) and on 1st September 2002 for all other purposes, replace regulations 37 to 42 of the Hydrocarbon Oil Regulations 1973 (S.I. 1973/1311) (“the Oil Regulations”).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Safety of Navigation) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations give effect to the provisions of Chapter V of the International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea 1974 (SOLAS) as amended in accordance with its Protocol of 1988 and by IMO resolution MSC.99(73).

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations, 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”).

items.title: The Plant Health (Forestry) (Phytophthora ramorum) (Great Britain) Order 2002
items.description: This Order implements measures to prevent the introduction and spread into Great Britain of the pest Phytophthora ramorum, a fungus which is not established in the United Kingdom. It regulates the landing and movement in and within Great Britain of forest trees and wood and bark from forest trees originating in the United States of America which has been identified as susceptible to the pest, known to cause Sudden Oak Death syndrome in certain species of oak, as well as harm to other plants. There are associated plant health Orders in England, Scotland and Wales also taking measures against Phytophthora ramorum.

items.title: The Veterinary Surgeons Act 1966 (Schedule 3 Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends Schedule 3 to the Veterinary Surgeons Act 1966.

items.title: The Durham and Chester-le-Street Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Durham and Chester-le-Street Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The Stakeholder Pension Schemes (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 12 of the Stakeholder Pension Schemes Regulations 2000 (S.I. 2000/1403). That Regulation 12 provides for an annual declaration as to the arrangements made by the trustees or manager of a stakeholder scheme for ensuring the proper administration of the scheme and for those trustees or that manager to obtain statements from the scheme’s reporting accountant as to the reasonableness of the annual declaration. The trustees or manager are, prior to the coming into force of these Regulations, not required to make an annual declaration or obtain reporting accountant’s statements before 5th July 2002.

items.title: The Office of Communications Act 2002 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st July 2002 all the provisions of the Office of Communications Act 2002 not yet in force, except for section 1(7) and (8) and paragraph 12 of the Schedule. Sections 4 to 7 came into force on Royal Assent.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Dorset) (District of Purbeck) Order 2002
items.description: This Order designates the district of Purbeck, excluding certain roads and parking places as set out in Article 3 of this Order, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Dorset) (District of East Dorset) Order 2002 (revoked)
items.description: This Order designates the district of East Dorset, excluding certain roads as set out in Article 3 of this Order, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area. It also modifies Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991 in relation to the designated area to provide that parking offences in off-street car parks will not be decriminalised under the provisions of this Order.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Dorset) (District of West Dorset) Order 2002 (revoked)
items.description: This Order designates the district of West Dorset, with the exception of the A35 trunk road, together with its on and off slip roads, for its entire length in the district, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area. It also modifies Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991 in relation to the designated area to provide that parking offences in off-street car parks will not be decriminalised under the provisions of this Order.

items.title: The Street Works (Reinstatement) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Street Works (Reinstatement) Regulations 1992 (SI. 1992/1689) (the “1992 Regulations”) so far as they apply in England. The 1992 Regulations prescribed requirements for the specification of materials to be used and the standard of workmanship to be observed by undertakers in reinstating streets by reference to a Code of Practice issued in 1992. The Secretary of State has approved the issue of a new Code of Practice entitled “Specification for the Reinstatement of Openings in Highways” (ISBN No 0-11-552538-6) (the “new Code”) and these Regulations change those references in the 1992 Regulations to paragraphs which have been renumbered in the new Code. The Regulations also follow the new Code by providing that the Code applies to all roads carrying up to 125 million standard axles. Although, apart from this, these regulations only change paragraph numbers, the new Code introduces several new requirements, notably relating to the use of specific materials for reinstatement of the highway and introducing a new road category for very heavily trafficked roads.

items.title: The Gas (Connection Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: The Gas (Connection Charges) Regulations 2001 make provision for entitling a gas transporter to require a person requiring a connection to a relevant main in pursuance of section 10(2) (duty to connect certain premises) of the Gas Act 1986 to pay to the transporter an amount in respect of the expenses of the laying of the main to provide the connection to a relevant main. The regulations make such provision in respect of mains laid either before or after the commencement of the Regulations.

items.title: The St. Helens and Knowsley Community Health National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves on 1st April 2002:

items.title: The Craven, Harrogate and Rural District Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Craven, Harrogate and Rural District Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The South Buckinghamshire National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the South Buckinghamshire National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1992, which established the South Buckinghamshire National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The South Tees Acute Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order further amends the South Tees Acute Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1991, which established the South Tees Acute Hospitals National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The Community Healthcare Bolton National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves on 1st April 2002:

items.title: The Care Standards Act 2000 (Commencement No. 14 (England) and Transitional, Savings and Amendment Provisions) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings into force, in relation to England only, certain provisions of the Care Standards Act 2000 which amend other statutory provisions (article 3). They amend the Protection of Children Act 1999, to allow certain authorities to refer persons for inclusion in the list of individuals considered unsuitable to work with children. They also amend provisions in the Children Act 1989 concerning persons disqualified from carrying on or being employed in children’s homes and related appeals.

items.title: The Camden and Islington Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Camden and Islington Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 2001 (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The Eastbourne and County National Health Service Trust Change of Name and (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Eastbourne and County National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1992, which established the Eastbourne and County National Health Service Trust, and changes the name of that trust.

items.title: The Cheshire Community Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves on 1st April 2002:

items.title: The North Mersey Community National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves on 1st April 2002:

items.title: The South Essex Mental Health and Community Care National Health Service Trust Change of Name and (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the South Essex Mental Health and Community Care National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 2000, which established the South Essex Mental Health and Community Care National Health Service Trust, and changes the name of that trust.

items.title: The Chester and Halton Community National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves on 1st April 2002:

items.title: The Darlington Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Darlington Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the trust’s establishment and operational dates (article 2), the area for which it is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its membership (article 4), and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 5). It also makes provision for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 6).

items.title: The CommuniCare National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves on 1st April 2002:
