These Rules also remove from the governor the power to impose any additional days as a punishment on a prisoner found guilty by him, and add to his powers in certain other respects.

items.title: The Young Offender Institution (Amendment) Rules 2002
items.description: These Rules amend the Young Offender Institution Rules 2000 by providing for an adjudicator, approved by the Secretary of State, to inquire into charges of serious offences against discipline set out in those Rules. Where the governor determines that a charge is sufficiently serious, he must refer it to the adjudicator, who is to inquire into the offence no later than 28 days after it has been referred. At an inquiry into a charge that has been referred to the adjudicator, the inmate who has been charged is given the opportunity to be legally represented. If the adjudicator finds an inmate guilty, he has the power to impose upon him any punishment which the governor can impose, and can also impose an award of up to 42 additional days to be served in a young offender institution. These Rules also remove from the governor the power to impose any additional days as a punishment on an inmate found guilty by him, and add to his powers in certain other respects.

items.title: The Hamilton Oxford Schools Partnership Education Action Zone (Extension) Order 2002
items.description: This Order extends the Hamilton Oxford Schools Partnership Education Action Zone for a further period of two years.

items.title: The Greenwich-Time to Succeed Education Action Zone (Extension) Order 2002
items.description: This Order extends the Greenwich-Time to Succeed Education Action Zone for a further period of two years.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Hours of Work) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations implement—

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”) in relation to the suppression of radio interference, by amending Regulation 60 and Schedule 2, Table II in various respects.

items.title: The Terrorism Act 2000 (Cessation of Effect of Section 76) Order 2002
items.description: This Order provides that section 76 of the Terrorism Act shall cease to have effect. Section 76 deals with the admissibility in a trial on indictment for a scheduled offence of a statement made by the accused. The effect of section 76 ceasing to have effect is that the provisions of section 74 of the Police and Criminal Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989 No. 1341) will apply where previously section 76 of the Terrorism Act 2000 would have applied.

items.title: The Greater London Magistrates' Courts Authority (Pensions) Order 2002
items.description: Section 1 of the Superannuation Act 1972 (under which the Principal Civil Service Pension Scheme, the Civil Service Additional Voluntary Contribution Scheme and the Civil Service Compensation Scheme have been made) applies to employment and offices listed in Schedule 1 to that Act.

items.title: The A41 London to Birkenhead Trunk Road (Buckinghamshire) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A41 London to Birkenhead Trunk Road (Buckinghamshire) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Products of Animal Origin (Third Country Imports) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations update the reference to Commission Decision 2002/69/EC concerning certain protective measures with regard to the products of animal origin imported from China (OJ No. L30, 31.1.2002, p. 50) in Section C of Part I of Schedule 2 to the Products of Animal Origin (Third Country Imports) (England) Regulations 2002 (S.I. 2002/1227), by including in that reference references to the two Decisions which amend Decision 2002/69/EC, namely Commission Decision 2002/441/EC (OJ No, L151, 11.6.2002, p. 16) and Commission Decision 2002/573/EC (OJ No. L181, 11.7.2002, p.21).

items.title: The Disease Control (Interim Measures) (England) (No. 2) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, which applies to England only revokes and replaces (with amendments) the Disease Control (Interim Measures)(England) Order 2002 (S.I. 2002/242, as amended by S.I. 2002/907, S.I. 2002/1348 and S.I. 2002/1764) (the “DC Order”).

items.title: The Sheep and Goats Identification and Movement (Interim Measures) (England) (No. 2) Order 2002 (revoked)
items.description: This Order, which extends to England only and ceases to have effect on 1st February 2003, revokes and substantially remakes, the Sheep and Goats Identification and Movement (Interim Measures) (England) Order 2002 (S.I. 2000/240) (implementing the provisions relating to sheep and goats of the Council Directive 92/102/EEC on the identification and registration of animals (O.J. No. L355, 5.12.92 p.32)).

items.title: The Pigs (Records, Identification and Movement)(Interim Measures) (England) (No. 2) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, which extends to England and ceases to have effect on 1st February 2003, temporarily dis-applies and replaces the Pigs (Records, Identification and Movement) Order 1995 (S.I. 1995/11, as amended) (“the 1995 Order”). It consolidates (with amendments) the temporary changes to the 1995 Order, made by the Pigs (Records, Identification and Movement) (Interim Measures) (England) Order 2002 (S.I. 2002/241) (the 2002 Order).

items.title: The Travel Documents (Fees) (Amendments) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Travel Documents (Fees) Regulations 1999 (“the 1999 Regulations”). The 1999 Regulations prescribed fees for the making of applications for different types of travel document. A fee of £28 is payable upon the making of an application to the Secretary of State for a travel document under Article 28 of the 1951 Convention relating to the Status of Refugees and Article 28 of the 1954 Convention relating to the Status of Stateless Persons. In respect of applications for all other Home Office travel documents a fee of £67 is payable.

items.title: The Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Financial Promotion) (Amendment) (Electronic Commerce Directive) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Financial Promotion) Order 2001 (S.I. 2001/1335) (“the Financial Promotion Order”), and the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Promotion of Collective Investment Schemes) (Exemptions) Order 2001 (S.I. 2001/1060) (“the Scheme Promotion Order”) to take account of the European Parliament and Council Directive of 8 June 2000 on certain legal aspects of information society services, in particular electronic commerce, in the Internal market (No. 2000/31/EC) (“the Directive”).

items.title: The Tax Credits (Claims) (Transitional Provision) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order corrects an error in article 3 of the Tax Credits Act 2002 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2002 (S.I. 2002/1727 (C. 52)) which makes transitional provision in relation to claims for tax credit for the tax year 2003–04. The correction makes clear that such claims shall be treated as claims for the tax year 2002–03, so that the previous year income for such claims is income for the tax year 2001–02.

items.title: The A423 Maidenhead to Oxford Trunk Road (A34 Hinksey Hill Interchange to A4142 Heyford Hill Roundabout) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A423 Maidenhead to Oxford Trunk Road (A34 Hinksey Hill Interchange to A4142 Heyford Hill Roundabout) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A44 London to Aberystwyth Trunk Road (A34 Peartree Roundabout to A40 Wolvercote Roundabout) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A44 London to Aberystwyth Trunk Road (A34 Peartree Roundabout to A40 Wolvercote Roundabout) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Working Tax Credit (Payment by Employers) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to the payment of working tax credit under the Tax Credits Act 2002 (c. 21) by employers to their employees from 6th April 2003 onwards.

items.title: The Tax Credits (Payments by the Commissioners) Regulations 2002
items.description: Section 24(6) of the Tax Credits Act 2002 (c. 21) (“the Act”) provides that, subject to section 25 of the Act, payments of a tax credit must be made by the Commissioners of Inland Revenue (“the Board”). Section 25 of the Act provides for regulations to be made requiring employers to pay working tax credit.

items.title: The Health and Safety (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the regulations relating to health and safety at work which are specified below, to give effect in Great Britain to European requirements. They also contain drafting changes.

items.title: The Offshore Safety (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the definition of “offshore installation” in regulation 3 of the Offshore Installations and Pipeline Works (Management and Administration) Regulations 1995 by extending it to include a supplementary unit (as defined) and by modifying the reference in regulation 3(2) (excepted structures) to a mobile structure which has been taken out of use (regulation 2).

items.title: The Notification of New Substances (Amendment) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Notification of New Substances Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/3050) by amending Schedules 2 and 3 to incorporate a reduced test method for chemical intermediates thereby implementing paragraphs 7 and 8 of article 1 of Commission Directive 2001/59/EC (OJ No. L 225, 21.8.2001, p.1) adapting to technical progress for the 28th time Council Directive 67/548/EEC (OJ No. 196, 16.8.1967, p.1) (OJ/SE 1967 p.234) on the approximation of the laws, regulations and administrative provisions relating to the classification, packaging and labelling of dangerous substances.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Essex) (Borough of Brentwood) Order 2002
items.description: This Order applies to the whole of the Borough of Brentwood with the exception of the following roads:

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Essex) (Borough of Chelmsford) Order 2002
items.description: This Order applies to the whole of the Borough of Chelmsford with the exception of the following roads:

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Essex) (District of Epping Forest) Order 2002
items.description: This Order applies to the whole of the District of Epping Forest with the exception of the following roads:

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Essex) (Borough of Colchester) Order 2002 (revoked)
items.description: This Order applies to the whole of the Borough of Colchester with the exception of the following roads:

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Hampshire) (Borough of Basingstoke and Deane) Order 2002
items.description: This Order designates the borough of Basingstoke and Deane, excluding certain roads as set out in Article 3 of this Order, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (Metropolitan Borough of Bury) Order 2002
items.description: This Order designates the metropolitan borough of Bury, excluding certain roads as set out in Article 3 of this Order, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Football (Disorder) (Amendment) Act 2002 (Commencement) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings section 1 of the Football (Disorder) (Amendment) Act 2002 into force on 28th August 2002. The remainder of the Act came into force on Royal Assent.

items.title: The Fishing Vessels (Safety of 15–24 Metre Vessels) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations provide for fishing vessels of at least 15 metres length overall but less than 24 metres registered length to comply with the requirements of the “Code of Safe Working Practice for the Construction and Use of 15 metre (LOA) to less than 24 metre (L) Fishing Vessels” (“the Code of Practice”) published by the Maritime and Coastguard Agency (regulations 3 and 4).

items.title: The National Health Service Reform and Health Care Professions Act 2002 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2002
items.description: This Order is the first Commencement Order made under the National Health Service Reform and Health Care Professions Act 2002 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Social Security Amendment (Students and Income-related Benefits) (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814), the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971), the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967) and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207) in so far as those Regulations apply to students.

items.title: Fur Farming (Compensation Scheme) (England) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, which applies to England only, revokes and replaces the Fur Farming (Compensation) (England) Order 2001 (S.I. 2001/3853).

items.title: The Education (Governors' Annual Reports) (England) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Governors' Annual Reports) (England) Regulations 1999. The amendment permits the governors' annual report to be combined with any other document which the governing body is required to prepare by or under any other enactment.

items.title: The Care Standards Act 2000 (Commencement No. 16) (England) Order 2002
items.description: The Order brings into force on 2nd September 2002 in relation to England only section 79(1) of the Care Standards Act 2000 in order to give effect to the following provisions of Part XA of the Children Act 1989. (Part XA was added to the Children Act 1989 by the Care Standards Act 2000):

items.title: The Disability Discrimination Codes of Practice (Education) (Appointed Day) Order 2002
items.description: This Order appoints 1st September 2002 as the day on which the Disability Rights Commission Codes of Practice for Schools and for providers of Post-16 education and related services (both of which were issued on 8th July 2002) come into effect.

items.title: The Special Educational Needs and Disability Act 2001 (Commencement No. 5) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st September 2002 the remaining provisions, except as specified below, of the Special Educational Needs and Disability Act 2001 which are not in force at the date of this Order so that no further Order is required in relation to England and Scotland.

items.title: The Supreme Court Fees (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Supreme Court Fees Order 1999.

items.title: The Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) (No. 2) Order 2002
items.description:
This Order which is made under section 2(1) of the School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1991 (“the Act”) refers to a Document entitled “School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Document 2002” published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office, which is obtainable from Government book shops and through booksellers. It can also be found on the internet at 
www.teachernet.gov.uk/Management/pay-and-performance/pay/teacherspay/2002/.

items.title: The Income Tax (Sub-contractors in the Construction Industry) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Tax (Sub-contractors in the Construction Industry) Regulations (S.I. 1993/743, relevantly amended by S.I. 1998/2622 and 2001/1531). The effect of these Regulations is (a) to allow for the renewal of sub-contractors' temporary registration cards (CIS4(T)), (b) extend the period of validity of temporary registration cards from three to twelve months (or thirty six months if appropriate, which is intended to be used in exceptional cases), (c) allow for the use of system identifiers (“substitute identifying numbers”) on sub-contractors' gross payment certificates, and (d) to allow such certificates to be prepared by any authorised officer of the Commissioners of Inland Revenue (“the Board”) or by any person nominated by the Board.

items.title: The Long Residential Tenancies (Principal Forms) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend two forms which are prescribed for the purposes of Schedule 10 to the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (security of tenure on ending of long residential tenancies).

items.title: The County Court Fees (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the County Court Fees Order 1999.

items.title: The Public Trustee (Fees) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order makes the following amendments to the Public Trustee (Fees) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/855), which prescribes fees to be charged by the Public Trustee.1The Public Trustee’s power to compromise disputes in relation to fees or remit fees in simple or exceptional cases will no longer be subject to the approval of the Treasury.2For the purposes of calculating the administration fee, the estate will be valued as at 30th September 2002 (provided that the Public Trustee was acting on that date), instead of 30th September 1998.

items.title: The Witham, Braintree and Halstead Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Witham, Braintree and Halstead Care Trust (“the trust”), a Primary Care Trust under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. The trust is designated as a Care Trust under section 45 of the Health and Social Care Act 2001.

items.title: The Borough of Thurrock (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the borough of Thurrock. The recommendations were submitted to the Secretary of State, but the functions of the Secretary of State under sections 13 to 15 and 17 of the Local Government Act 1992 so far as they relate to electoral changes were transferred with modifications to the Electoral Commission under The Local Government Commission for England (Transfer of Functions) Order 2001 (S.I. 2001/3962), with effect from 1st April 2002. That Order provides that recommendations submitted to the Secretary of State before 1st April would have effect as if they had been submitted to the Electoral Commission.

items.title: The Borough of Medway (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect, with one modification, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the borough of Medway. The recommendations were submitted to the Secretary of State, but the functions of the Secretary of State under sections 13 to 15 and 17 of the Local Government Act 1992 so far as they relate to electoral changes were transferred with modifications to the Electoral Commission under the Local Government Commission for England (Transfer of Functions) Order 2001 (S.I. 2001/3962), with effect from 1st April 2002. That Order provides that recommendations submitted to the Secretary of State before 1st April would have effect as if they had been submitted to the Electoral Commission.

items.title: The City of Plymouth (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the city of Plymouth. The recommendations were submitted to the Secretary of State, but the functions of the Secretary of State under sections 13 to 15 and 17 of the Local Government Act 1992 so far as they relate to electoral changes were transferred with modifications to the Electoral Commission under The Local Government Commission for England (Transfer of Functions) Order 2001 (S.I. 2001/3962), with effect from 1st April 2002. That Order provides that recommendations submitted to the Secretary of State before 1st April would have effect as if they had been submitted to the Electoral Commission.

items.title: The Borough of Warrington (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: The Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the borough of Warrington. The recommendations were submitted to the Secretary of State, but the functions of the Secretary of State under sections 13 to 15 and 17 of the Local Government Act 1992 so far as they relate to electoral changes were transferred with modifications to the Electoral Commission under the Local Government Commission for England (Transfer of Functions) Order 2001 (S.I. 2001/3962), with effect from 1st April 2002. That Order provides that recommendations submitted to the Secretary of State before 1st April would have effect as if they had been submitted to the Electoral Commission.

items.title: The District of East Dorset (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Boundary Committee for England, a committee of the Electoral Commission, for electoral changes in the district of East Dorset.

items.title: The District of North Dorset (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Boundary Committee for England, a committee of the Electoral Commission, for electoral changes in the district of North Dorset.

items.title: The Elections (Policy Development Grants Scheme) Order 2002
items.description: This Order makes provision for a Scheme, set out in the Schedule to the Order, for the making, by the Electoral Commission, of grants to political parties (“policy development grants”) to assist the parties with the development of policies for inclusion in their manifestos for parliamentary elections, elections to the European Parliament, the Scottish Parliament, the National Assembly for Wales, and the Northern Ireland Assembly, local government elections and local elections in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Borough of Blackpool (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Boundary Committee for England, a committee of the Electoral Commission, for electoral changes in the borough of Blackpool. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The Borough of Christchurch (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect, with one modification, to recommendations by the Boundary Committee for England, a committee of the Electoral Commission, for electoral changes in the borough of Christchurch.

items.title: The Borough of Halton (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: The Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the borough of Halton. The recommendations were submitted to the Secretary of State, but the functions of the Secretary of State under sections 13 to 15 and 17 of the Local Government Act 1992 so far as they relate to electoral changes were transferred with modifications to the Electoral Commission under the Local Government Commission for England (Transfer of Functions) Order 2001 (S.I. 2001/3962), with effect from 1st April 2002. That Order provides that recommendations submitted to the Secretary of State before 1st April would have effect as if they had been submitted to the Electoral Commission.

items.title: The District of West Berkshire (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect, with one modification, to recommendations by the Boundary Committee for England, a committee of the Electoral Commission, for electoral changes in the district of West Berkshire. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Goods and Services) (Public Bodies) (England) (No. 2) Order 2002
items.description: Article 2 of this Order designates Kirklees Active Leisure as a public body under section 1(5) of the Local Authorities (Goods and Services) Act 1970 (“the 1970 Act”).

items.title: The State Pension Credit Act (Commencement No. 2) Order 2002
items.description: This Order appoints 3rd September 2002 as the day for the coming into force of section 18 of the State Pension Credit Act 2002. Section 18 amends the Pension Schemes Act 1993 to provide for equal treatment for widows and widowers in connection with the treatment of their guaranteed minimum pensions.

items.title: The Statutory Maternity Pay (Compensation of Employers) Amendment Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Statutory Maternity Pay (Compensation of Employers) and Miscellaneous Amendment Regulations 1994 (“the principal GB Regulations”) and the Statutory Maternity Pay (Compensation of Employers) and Miscellaneous Amendment Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994 (“the principal NI Regulations”).

items.title: The Employment Act 2002 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings into force further provisions of the Employment Act 2002 (c. 22).

items.title: The Lancashire (Coroners' Districts) Order 2002
items.description: The County of Lancashire was divided into five coroners' districts by the Lancashire (Coroners' Districts) Order 1974. Two of these, Blackburn, Hyndburn and Ribble Valley and Blackpool and Fylde, ceased to be part of the jurisdiction of Lancashire by the Lancashire (Boroughs of Blackburn and Blackpool) (Structural Change) Order 1996 and the Lancashire (Coroners) Order 1998, when the unitary authorities of Blackpool and Blackburn with Darwen were established.

items.title: The Forestry (Felling of Trees) (England and Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend as respects England and Wales the Forestry (Felling of Trees) Regulations 1979 (S.I. 1979/791, as amended by S.I. 1987/632). They insert a definition of “electronic communication” and a provision relating to references to the communication being “authenticated” to enable applications for felling licences to be made electronically. They also substitute a new regulation 4 and a new Schedule 2, which sets out particulars to be included in an application for a felling licence (instead of the previous requirement to complete a standard application form). They come into force on 1st March 2002.

items.title: The Housing (Scotland) Act 2001 (Housing Support Services Information) Order 2002
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential on the Housing (Scotland) Act 2001 (“the 2001 Act”).

items.title: The Tonnage Tax (Training Requirement) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Tonnage Tax (Training Requirement) Regulations 2000 by increasing the amount of the payments in lieu of training. In respect of a relevant four month period falling on or after 1st October 2002, the figure by which the number of months is to be multiplied is increased from £562 to £573. In the case of the higher rate of payment where there has been failure to meet the training requirement, the basic rate to be used in the calculations is increased from £512 to £522.

items.title: The Origin of Goods (Petroleum Products) (Revocation) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Origin of Goods (Petroleum Products) Regulations 1988. Those Regulations laid down the conditions under which certain petroleum products specified in the Schedule to the Regulations were to be treated as originating in a particular country for the purposes of preferential rates of, or exemption from, customs duties charged in accordance with section 5(1) and (2) of the European Communities Act 1972. At the time of the making of those Regulations there were no Community origin rules for these products.

items.title: The Trade Union Ballots and Elections (Independent Scrutineer Qualifications) Order 1993 (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: Sections 49, 75, 100A and 226B of the Trade Union and Labour Relations (Consolidation) Act 1992 require certain ballots and elections that trade unions are required to hold by statute to be supervised by a qualified independent person, known as a “scrutineer”, appointed by the trade union.

items.title: The Recognition and Derecognition Ballots (Qualified Persons) Order 2000 (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: Under Schedule A1 to the Trade Union and Labour Relations (Consolidation) Act 1992, where the Central Arbitration Committee arranges a ballot on the recognition or derecognition of a trade union for collective bargaining, it must appoint a “qualified independent person” to conduct the ballot.

items.title: The Birmingham and the Black Country Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Wolverhampton City Primary Care Trust, trust property held by the Birmingham and the Black Country Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”) in relation to in-service exhaust emissions tests as a result of the requirement to implement Commission Directive 2001/9/EC which amended Council Directive 96/96/EC (“the Roadworthiness Directive”).

items.title: The County Durham and Tees Valley Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Durham Dales Primary Care Trust, trust property held by the County Durham and Tees Valley Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Greater Manchester Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Central Manchester and Manchester Children’s University Hospitals National Health Service Trust, trust property held by the Greater Manchester Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Leicestershire, Northamptonshire and Rutland Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Northamptonshire Healthcare National Health Service Trust, trust property held by the Leicestershire, Northamptonshire and Rutland Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The North Central London Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Camden Primary Care Trust, trust property held by the North Central London Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The North West London Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Chelsea and Westminster Healthcare NHS Trust, trust property held by the North West London Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Northumberland, Tyne & Wear Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Gateshead Health National Health Service Trust, trust property held by the Northumberland, Tyne & Wear Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The South East London Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Greenwich Primary Care Trust, trust property held by the South East London Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The South West Peninsula Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Cornwall Partnership National Health Service Trust, trust property held by the South West Peninsula Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Thames Valley Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the South Buckinghamshire National Health Service Trust, the Stoke Mandeville Hospital National Health Service Trust and the Two Shires Ambulance National Health Service Trust, trust property held by the Thames Valley Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedules specifying the trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Trent Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Derbyshire Mental Health Services National Health Service Trust, the Doncaster and Bassetlaw Hospitals National Health Service Trust, the Lincolnshire Partnership National Health Service Trust, the Nottinghamshire Healthcare National Health Service Trust and the United Lincolnshire Hospitals National Health Service Trust, trust property held by the Trent Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedules specifying the trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Anti-terrorism, Crime and Security Act 2001 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2002
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into force on 14th February 2002 of Part 12 (bribery and corruption) of the Anti-terrorism, Crime and Security Act 2001.

items.title: The West Yorkshire Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Bradford District Care Trust and the Mid Yorkshire Hospitals National Health Service Trust, trust property held by the West Yorkshire Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedules specifying trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Avon, Gloucestershire and Wiltshire Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Gloucestershire Partnership National Health Service Trust and the Cotswold and Vale Primary Care Trust, trust property held by the Avon, Gloucestershire and Wiltshire Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedules specifying the trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Cumbria and Lancashire Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the Lancashire Teaching Hospitals National Health Service Trust and the Blackpool, Fylde and Wyre Hospitals National Health Service Trust, trust property held by the Cumbria and Lancashire Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedules specifying the trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Surrey and Sussex Health Authority (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the East Sussex County National Health Service Trust, the South Downs Health National Health Service Trust, the East Elmbridge and Mid Surrey Primary Care Trust, the Royal Surrey County Hospital National Health Service Trust and the Surrey Hampshire Borders National Health Service Trust and the North West Surrey Mental Health National Health Service Partnership Trust, trust property held by the Surrey and Sussex Health Authority and makes related provision. A copy of the schedules specifying the trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Courts-Martial (Royal Air Force) (Amendment) Rules 2002
items.description: These Rules amend the Courts-Martial (Royal Air Force) Rules 1997 (“the 1997 Rules”). The amendments are consequential on the provisions of the Armed Forces Act 2001 making warrant officers eligible in certain circumstances to sit as members of courts-martial and enabling courts-martial to require witnesses to produce things as well as documents.

items.title: The Mobile Telephones (Re-programming) Act 2002 (Commencement) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings sections 1 and 2 of the Mobile Telephones (Re-programming) Act 2002 into force on 4th October 2002.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Companies) (Amendment) (England) Order 2002
items.description: Part V of the Local Authorities (Companies) Order 1995 (“the 1995 Order”) applies the provisions of Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (revenue accounts and capital finance of local authorities), subject to modifications, to companies subject to the influence or control of local authorities. Articles 13 and 14 make provision for treating things done by or to such a company as if they were done by or to the local authority concerned, and in respect of such things (other than a reduction made in the company’s liabilities) require the authority to have available an amount of credit cover.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations make a number of amendments to the Local Authorities (Capital Finance) Regulations 1997 (“the 1997 Regulations”) resulting from the implementation of the Greater London Authority Act 1999.

items.title: The Courts-Martial (Army) (Amendment) Rules 2002
items.description: These Rules amend the Courts-Martial (Army) Rules 1997 (“the 1997 Rules”). The amendments are consequential on the provisions of the Armed Forces Act 2001 making warrant officers eligible in certain circumstances to sit as members of courts-martial and enabling courts-martial to require witnesses to produce things as well as documents.

items.title: The Disease Control (Interim Measures) (England) (No. 2) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Disease Control (Interim Measures) (England) (No. 2) Order 2002 (S.I. 2002/2152) (the “principal Order”).

items.title: The Education Action Forum (Proceedings) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the existing provisions which set out the proceedings for Education Action Forums. The quorum required for a meeting on any issue is reduced to one third of the Forum members or 6 people, whichever is the greater.

items.title: The Police Reform Act 2002 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings into force the provisions of the Police Reform Act 2002 set out in article 2 on 1st October 2002 either generally or to the extent specified.

items.title: The Courts-Martial (Royal Navy) (Amendment) Rules 2002
items.description: These Rules amend the Courts-Martial (Royal Navy) Rules 1997 (“the 1997 Rules”). The amendments are consequential on the provisions of the Armed Forces Act 2001 making warrant officers eligible in certain circumstances to sit as members of courts-martial and enabling courts-martial to require witnesses to produce things as well as documents.

items.title: The Gaming Duty (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Gaming Duty Regulations 1997 (S.I. 1997/2196), regulation 5, the amount of payments on account. They substitute a new Table reflecting changes to gaming duty made by section 10 of the Finance Act 2002 (c. 23), and will apply in the case of payments on account of gaming duty for any quarter that ends on or after 31st October 2002.

items.title: The Social Security (Incapacity) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (General Benefit) Regulations 1982, the Social Security (Incapacity Benefit) Regulations 1994 and the Social Security (Incapacity for Work) (General) Regulations 1995.

items.title: The Attestation of Constables (Welsh Language) Order 2002
items.description: This Order specifies the form of words in Welsh which may be used in police areas in Wales for the attestation of constables or special constables as an alternative to the words in Schedule 4 to the Police Act 1996.

items.title: The Police (Property) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police (Property) Regulations 1997 (“the 1997 Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security Amendment (Employment Programme) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207), the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971) and the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814).

items.title: The Beef Labelling (Enforcement) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend The Beef Labelling (Enforcement) (England) Regulations 2000 (S.I. 2000/3047).

items.title: The Education Act 2002 (Modification of Provisions) (England) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations make modifications in connection with the bringing into force of section 27 of the Education Act 2002 on 2nd September 2002, and in connection with the proposed bringing into force of sections 30, 37, and Part 6 of, and Schedule 1 to, the Education Act 2002 on 1st October 2002, relating to community facilities, governors' reports, payments in respect of dismissal etc., and the curriculum in England.

items.title: The Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 2001 (Amendment) (No. 4) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 2001 (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 2002, which contained a drafting defect.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) Amendment Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 which provide for a scheme whereby housing benefit is payable to persons who are liable to make certain payments in respect of a dwelling occupied as their home.

items.title: The Social Fund (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Fund (Applications) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/524) by allowing applications for crisis loans to be made otherwise than in writing and making consequential amendments relating to when such applications are to be treated as made (regulation 2).

items.title: The Homelessness Act 2002 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions) (England) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The Homelessness Act 2002 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions) (England) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 (Department of Trade and Industry Investigations) Order 2002
items.description: This Order vests in an investigator appointed by the Secretary of State for Trade and Industry, when investigating a serious arrestable offence or anything which there are reasonable grounds for suspecting has involved the commission of a serious arrestable offence, the powers set out in the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 (“the Act”) for a constable to apply to a circuit judge for (i) an order requiring production of or access to “special procedure material” or (ii) a warrant for one or more constables together with one or more DTI investigators to search for and seize such material. “Special procedure material” is defined in section 14 of the Act and “serious arrestable offence” is defined in section 116 of the Act. Article 4 of the Order makes consequential amendments.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Member-nominated Trustees and Directors) Amendment Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Occupational Pension Schemes (Member-nominated Trustees and Directors) Regulations 1996 (“the Member-nominated Trustees Regulations”) which concern the selection and appointment of member-nominated trustees and directors under sections 16 to 21 of the Pensions Act 1995.

items.title: The Police Act 1997 (Criminal Records) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations make detailed provisions in relation to applications for criminal record certificates and enhanced criminal record certificates under Part V of the Police Act 1997.

items.title: The Food (Peanuts from China) (Emergency Control) (England) (No. 2) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to England only, revoke and re-enact with changes the Food (Peanuts from China) (Emergency Control) (England) Regulations 2002. The revocation is effected by regulation 7.

items.title: The Food (Figs, Hazelnuts and Pistachios from Turkey) (Emergency Control) (England) (No. 2) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to England only, revoke and re-enact with changes the Food (Figs, Hazelnuts and Pistachios from Turkey) (Emergency Control) (England) Regulations 2002. The revocation is effected by regulation 7.

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) Regulations 2000 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Miscellaneous Dental Charges Amendments) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) Regulations 1988 (“the Travelling Expenses Regulations”) and the National Health Service (Dental Charges) Regulations 1989 (“the Dental Charges Regulations”).

items.title: The Lottery Duty (Instant Chances) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 17th October 2002, amend the Lottery Duty (Instant Chances) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/2815).

items.title: The Lottery Duty (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 17th October 2002, amend the Lottery Duty Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/3212).

items.title: The Bovines and Bovine Products (Trade) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Bovines and Bovine Products (Trade) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/1103, as amended by S.I. 1999/1554, S.I. 2000/656 and S.I. 2000/1667—“the Principal Regulations”) insofar as they extend to England. The Principal Regulations give effect to Commission Decisions 98/692/EC (OJ No. L328, 4.12.98, p. 28) and 98/564/EC (OJ No. L273, 9.10.98, p. 37) which amended Council Decision 98/256/EC (OJ No. L113, 15.4.98, p.32).

items.title: The Medicines (Codification Amendments Etc.) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations make consequential amendments to the references in the Medicines Act 1968 and various statutory instruments relating to medicinal products and devices following the adoption of Directive 2001/83/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on the Community code relating to medicinal products for human use. That Codification Directive repealed and re-enacted Council Directive 65/65/EEC (and its successive amendments, namely Council Directives 66/454/EEC, 75/319/EEC, 83/570/EEC, 87/21/EEC, 89/341/EEC, 92/27/EEC and 93/39/EEC); Council Directive 75/318/EEC (and its successive amendments, namely Council Directives 83/570/EEC, 87/19/EEC, 89/341/EEC, 91/507/EEC and 93/39/EEC and Commission Directives 1999/82/EC and 1999/83/EC); Council Directive 75/319/EEC (and its successive amendments, namely Council Directives 78/420/EEC, 83/570/EEC, 89/341/EEC, 92/27/EEC and 93/39/EEC and Commission Directive 2000/38/EC); Council Directive 89/342/EEC; Council Directive 89/343/EEC; Council Directive 89/381/EEC; Council Directive 92/25/EEC; Council Directive 92/26/EEC; Council Directive 92/27/EEC; Council Directive 92/28/EEC and Council Directive 92/73/EEC. Where references to the repealed Directives appeared in the Medicines Act 1968 and statutory instruments numbered 1971/972, 1971/974, 1976/1726, 1992/3146, 1993/2538, 1994/105, 1994/1932, 1994/1933, 1994/2987, 1994/3017, 1994/3144, 1995/309, 1995/449, 1995/1116, 2000/123, 2000/1763, 2001/1422 and 2001/3798, they have been replaced with an appropriate reference to the 2001 Codification Directive and related legislation.

items.title: The Health and Social Care Act 2001 (Commencement No. 10) (England) Order 2002
items.description: This Order is the 10th commencement order made under the Health and Social Care Act 2001 (“the Act”), and applies to England only.

items.title: The Plastic Materials and Articles in Contact with Food (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to England only, amend for the second time the Plastic Materials and Articles in Food Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998/1376, “the 1998 Regulations”), which extend to the whole of Great Britain. The Regulations implement—Commission Directive 2001/62/EC amending Directive 90/128/EEC relating to plastic materials and articles intended to come into contact with foodstuffs (OJ No. L221, 17.8.2001, p.18);Commission Directive 2002/16/EC on the use of certain epoxy derivatives in materials and articles intended to come into contact with foodstuffs (OJ No. L51, 22.2.2002, p.27); and(in minor respects) Commission Directive 2002/17/EC amending Directive 90/128/EEC relating to plastic materials and articles intended to come into contact with foodstuffs (OJ No. L58, 28.2.2002, p.19).

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment No. 3) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 2001 (S.I. 2001/1004) (“the principal Regulations”). They also make consequential amendments to the Social Security (Crediting and Treatment of Contributions, and National Insurance Numbers) Regulations 2001 (S.I. 2001/769) and the corresponding provision in Northern Ireland (S.R. 2001 No. 102). The amendment provide for decisions to be taken by officers of the Board and accordingly appealable to the tax appeal Commissioners under section 11 of the Social Security Contributions (Transfer of Functions, etc.) Act 1999 (c. 2) (“the Transfer Act”) or Article 10 of the Social Security Contributions (Transfer of Functions, etc.) (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/671) (“the Transfer Order”).

items.title: The Housing (Scotland) Act 2001 (Accommodation for Asylum-Seekers) Order 2002
items.description: This Order provides that a tenancy granted to asylum-seekers under Part VI of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999 is not a Scottish secure tenancy within the meaning of the Housing (Scotland) Act 2001, unless the tenant is notified otherwise by the landlord.

items.title: The Borough of Weymouth and Portland (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Boundary Committee for England, a committee of the Electoral Commission, for electoral changes in the borough of Weymouth and Portland.

items.title: The City of Cambridge (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Boundary Committee for England, a committee of the Electoral Commission, for electoral changes in the City of Cambridge. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 6th May 2004.

items.title: The Road Traffic (NHS Charges) Amendment Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Road Traffic (NHS Charges) Regulations 1999 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for a scheme for the recovery from insurers and certain other persons of charges in connection with the treatment of road traffic casualties by the National Health Service (NHS). They also revoke the Road Traffic (NHS Charges) Amendment Regulations 2001 (regulation 4).

items.title: The District of West Dorset (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Boundary Committee for England, a committee of the Electoral Commission, for electoral changes in the district of West Dorset.

items.title: The Borough of Bracknell Forest (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Boundary Committee for England, a committee of the Electoral Commission, for electoral changes in the borough of Bracknell Forest.

items.title: The Royal Borough of Windsor and Maidenhead (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect, with one modification, to recommendations by the Boundary Committee for England, a committee of the Electoral Commission, for electoral changes in the Royal Borough of Windsor and Maidenhead.

items.title: The Borough of Telford and Wrekin (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect, with one modification, to recommendations by the Boundary Committee for England, a committee of the Electoral Commission, for electoral changes in the borough of Telford and Wrekin. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The District of South Cambridgeshire (Electoral Changes) Order 2002
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Boundary Committee for England, a committee of the Electoral Commission, for electoral changes in the district of South Cambridgeshire. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 6th May 2004.

items.title: The National Health Service (Functions of Strategic Health Authorities and Primary Care Trusts and Administration Arrangements) (England) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations replace the existing Regulations relating to the functions of Health Authorities and Primary Care Trusts in England in the light of the provisions of the National Health Service Reform and Health Care Professions Act 2002 (c. 17) which rename Health Authorities in England as Strategic Health Authorities and allow for direct delegation of the Secretary of State’s functions to Primary Care Trusts.

items.title: The Council for the Regulation of Health Care Professionals (Appointment etc.) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations make provision concerning the appointment, etc. of the members and chairman of the Council for the Regulation of Health Care Professionals established by section 25 of the National Health Service Reform and Health Care Professions Act 2002.

items.title: The Vehicles (Crime) Act 2001 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2002
items.description: This Order commences sections 32 of the Vehicle (Crime) Act 2001 on 17th September 2002 and 33 of the same Act on the same date together with consequential provisions. Section 32 amends and clarifies provisions regarding applications for registration of motor vehicles. Section 33 confers a power to make regulations providing that the Secretary of State may refuse a new registration document where he is not satisfied that the vehicle for which it is sought is the registered vehicle.

items.title: The Driving Licences (Exchangeable Licences) Order 2002
items.description: This Order designates the Republic of Korea and the Principality of Monaco under section 108(2)(b) of the Road Traffic Act 1988 as making satisfactory provisions for the issue of certain classes of driving licence. This enables a person holding one of these licences to exchange it for a corresponding British licence.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 2001 (S.I. 2001/1004) (“the principal Regulations”). Following that consolidation, the opportunity is being taken to combine the relevant annual changes to National Insurance Contributions for Great Britain and Northern Ireland in a single instrument.

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments) (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207), the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971) and the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2002
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1973 in order to—

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: This version of the The Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) (Amendment) Regulations 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Eastbourne and County National Health Service Trust Change of Name and (Establishment) Amendment Order (No. 2) 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Eastbourne and County National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1992, which established the Eastbourne and County National Health Service Trust (“the trust”). The name of the trust was changed from the Eastbourne and County National Health Service Trust to the East Sussex County National Health Service Trust by the Eastbourne and County National Health Service Trust Change of Name and (Establishment) Amendment Order 2002.

items.title: The Strand Road, Preston Railway Order 2002
items.description: This Order relates to a portion of the Preston Dock branch railway (also known as the Ribble branch railway) in the vicinity of, and including, the Strand Road, Preston level crossing which at the date of the Order is owned jointly by Preston City Council and Railtrack PLC (in Railway Administration). The Order authorises the owners to transfer the railway to Steamport Southport Ltd and makes other provisions in relation to it.

items.title: The Sheep and Goats Identification and Movement (Interim Measures) (England) Order 2002 (revoked)
items.description: (This Note does not form part of the Order)

items.title: The Coroners' Records (Fees for Copies) Rules 2002
items.description: These Rules, which replace the Coroners' Records (Fees for Copies) Rules 1997, increase (in line with inflation) the fees payable to coroners or other persons for copies of inquisitions, depositions or other documents relating to inquests.

items.title: The Income-related Benefits and Jobseeker’s Allowance (Working Tax Credit and Child Tax Credit) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967) (Schedule 1 to these Regulations), the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207) (Schedule 2 to these Regulations), the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations (S.I. 1992/1814) (Schedule 3 to these Regulations) and the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (S.I. 1987/1971) (Schedule 4 to these Regulations) referred to in this note as “the amended Regulations”. They make provision to the amended Regulations in connection with the introduction of child tax credit and working tax credit by the Tax Credits Act 2002 (“the 2002 Act”).

items.title: The M60 Motorway (Junction 25) (Speed Limit) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations impose a 50 miles per hour speed limit on the clockwise carriageway of the M60 between Marker Posts 52.3 and 53.5.

items.title: The African Development Fund (Additional Subscriptions) Order 2002
items.description: This Order approves the making of a payment on behalf of the Government of the United Kingdom of a sum not exceeding £40,000,000 as a further contribution to the African Development Fund in accordance with the arrangements made with the Fund pursuant to Resolution F/BG/2002/03 adopted by the Board of Governors of the Fund on 28th May 2002.

items.title: The Caribbean Development Bank (Further Payments) Order 2002
items.description: This Order approves the making of a payment on behalf of the Government of the United Kingdom of a sum not exceeding £17,496,000 as a further contribution to the Special Development Fund of the Caribbean Development Bank in accordance with the arrangements made pursuant to the Resolution entitled “Resolution of Contributors to the Special Development Fund of the Caribbean Development Bank (SDF V)” adopted by the Board of Directors on 13th December 2001.

items.title: The Controlled Foreign Companies (Excluded Countries) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Controlled Foreign Companies (Excluded Countries) Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998/3081: “the 1998 Regulations”).

items.title: The Pigs (Records, Identification and Movement) (Interim Measures) (England) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, which extends to England, provides that the Pigs (Records, Identification and Movement) Order 1995 (S.I. 1995/11, as amended) (“the principal Order”) shall be read for the specified period as if amended in certain respects.

items.title: The Buckinghamshire Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the South Buckinghamshire National Health Service Trust and Stoke Mandeville Hospital National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the Buckinghamshire Hospitals National Health Service Trust (“the new trust”) as a National Health Service Trust under the terms set out in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 (article 2).

items.title: The Disease Control (Interim Measures) (England) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, which applies to England only, provides for temporary disease control measures.

items.title: The County Durham and Darlington Acute Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the North Durham Health Care National Health Service Trust and South Durham Health Care National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order establishes the County Durham and Darlington Acute Hospitals National Health Service Trust (“the trust”) as a National Health Service Trust under the terms set out in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 (article 2).

items.title: The Aerodromes (Designation) (Facilities for Consultation) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: Under section 35 of the Civil Aviation Act 1982, the person having management of any aerodrome to which the section applies is required to provide facilities for consultation on matters concerning the management or administration of the aerodrome for users of the aerodrome, for any appropriate local authority and for any other organisation representing local interests.

items.title: The A595 Trunk Road (Lillyhall to A66 Chapel Brow) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A595 Trunk Road (Lillyhall to A66 Chapel Brow) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A6 Trunk Road (Levens Slip Road) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A6 Trunk Road (Levens Slip Road) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A590 Trunk Road (Levens Slip Road) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A590 Trunk Road (Levens Slip Road) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A69 Trunk Road (M6 to Carlisle City Boundary) (Detrunking) Order 2002
items.description: This version of the The A69 Trunk Road (M6 to Carlisle City Boundary) (Detrunking) Order 2002 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Road Vehicles (Testing) (Disclosure of Information) (Great Britain) Regulations 2002
items.description: These regulations complete the transposition in Great Britain of Directive 2000/30 of the European Parliament and of the Council, allowing the Secretary of State as competent authority in Great Britain to give information obtained in the course of testing and inspecting commercial vehicles for their roadworthiness to be passed to the competent authorities in other member States and in Northern Ireland and Gibraltar. Other provisions of the Directive are already incorporated as respects Great Britain under sections 66A to 73 of the Road Traffic Act 1988, the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 the Road Vehicles Lighting Regulations 1989 and the Road Traffic (Foreign Vehicles) Act 1972.

items.title: The National Heritage (Territorial Waters Adjacent to England) Order 2002
items.description: Sections 33(9) and 33(10) of the National Heritage Act 1983, which were inserted by the National Heritage Act 2002, give the Historic Buildings and Monuments Commission for England (English Heritage) power to preserve and to promote the public’s enjoyment of ancient monuments which are in, on or under the sea bed, within the seaward limits of the United Kingdom territorial waters which are adjacent to England, such boundaries to be determined by the Secretary of State. The Order determines for the purposes of section 33(9) the boundaries between waters which are to be treated as internal waters or territorial sea of the United Kingdom adjacent to England and those which are not.

items.title: The Broomfield Agricultural College (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 1st March 2002 the further education corporation established to conduct the Broomfield Agricultural College. It provides for the transfer of its property, rights and liabilities to Derby College and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The Education Act 2002 (Commencement No. 2 and Savings and Transitional Provisions) Order 2002
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Education Act 2002 on 1st October 2002. The provisions are listed in articles 2, 3 and 4.

items.title: The Mackworth College (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 1st March 2002 the further education corporation established to conduct the Mackworth College. It provides for the transfer of its property, rights and liabilities to Derby College and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Essex) (Borough of Brentwood) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Essex) (Borough of Brentwood) Order 2002 (“the principal order”) for the purpose of changing the area to which that Order applies. Regulation 3 of the principal order provides that the principal order applies to the Borough of Chelmsford. This Order substitutes for the words “the Borough of Chelmsford” the words “the Borough of Brentwood”.

items.title: The Social Security (Claims and Payments and Miscellaneous Amendments) (No. 2) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1987 (regulations 2 to 13) and the Social Security (Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery) Regulations 1988 (regulation 14) to make further provision as to the payment of social security benefits.

items.title: The Social Security Amendment (Personal Injury Payments) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814), the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971), the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967) and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207).

items.title: Genetically Modified Organisms (Deliberate Release) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations implement, in respect of England and the United Kingdom sector of the continental shelf, Council Directive 2001/18/EC on the deliberate release into the environment of genetically modified organisms (“the Directive”) which replaced Council Directive 1990/220/EEC (as amended) of the same title.

items.title: The Derby Tertiary College-Wilmorton (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 1st March 2002 the further education corporation established to conduct the Derby Tertiary College-Wilmorton. It provides for the transfer of its property, rights and liabilities to Derby College and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The East Yorkshire College of Further Education, Bridlington (Dissolution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 1st March 2002 the further education corporation established to conduct East Yorkshire College of Further Education, Bridlington. It provides for the transfer of its property, rights and liabilities to Beverley College of Further Education (to be known from 1st March 2002 as East Riding College) and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The General Dental Council (Election of Members) Rules Order of Council 2002
items.description: This Order, which is made under the Dentists Act 1984, approves Rules made by the General Dental Council setting out arrangements for the election of the members of the Council as constituted under section 1(2A) of that Act.

items.title: The General Dental Council (President of the Council) Rules Order of Council 2002
items.description: This Order, which is made under the Dentists Act 1984, approves Rules made by the General Dental Council setting out arrangements for the election of the President of the Council as constituted under section 1(2A) of that Act.

items.title: The Education (Nursery Education and Early Years Development) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Nursery Education and Early Years Development) (England) Regulations 1999 which set out the minimum age in relation to which a local education authority must secure sufficient provision of nursery education for their area and make provision in connection with the early years development plan which each authority is required to prepare.

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No. 2)Order 2002
items.description: This order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, safety zones having a radius of 500 metres from the specified point around each installation specified in the Schedule to this order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)) (article 2).

items.title: The Metropolitan Police Authority (Civil Staff Pensions) Order 2002
items.description: This Order transfers to the principal civil service pension scheme (“the PCSPS”) certain members and beneficiaries of the pension arrangements having effect by virtue of section 15(2) of the Superannuation (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 1967, which are known as the Metropolitan Civil Staffs Superannuation Scheme (“the MCSSS”). The members and beneficiaries covered by the Order are persons employed or formerly employed by the Metropolitan Police Authority (or one of its predecessors) otherwise than as constables and their dependants.

items.title: The National Health Service Reform and Health Care Professions Act 2002 (Supplementary, Consequential etc. Provisions) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations make amendments consequential on those sections of the National Health Service Reform and Health Care Professions Act 2002 (“the Act”) concerned with the structure of the NHS in England and in particular on the renaming of Health Authorities as Strategic Health Authorities by section 1 of the Act and the reallocation of many of the existing functions of Health Authorities to Primary Care Trusts by section 2 of, and Schedule 2 to, the Act.

items.title: The Patents and Plant Variety Rights (Compulsory Licensing) Regulations 2002
items.description: (This note does not form part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Brightlingsea Harbour Revision (Constitution) Order 2002
items.description: This Order reconstitutes the Brightlingsea Harbour Commissioners as from 1st October 2002. It provides for the Commissioners to consist of a body of eight Commissioners with experience in relevant matters. Six persons will be appointed by the Commissioners and the chief executive and the harbour master will also hold office as Commissioners. Under the terms of the Order the appointed Commissioners will retire in rotation.

items.title: The National Health Service Reform and Health Care Professions Act 2002 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2002
items.description: This Order is the second Commencement Order made under the National Health Service Reform and Health Care Professions Act 2002 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Dangerous Substances and Preparations (Safety) (Consolidation)(Amendment No. 2) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement European Parliament and Council Directive 2001/41/EC (O.J. No. L194, 18.07.01, p.36), which amended for the 21st time Council Directive 76/769/EEC (O.J. No. L262, 27.9.76, p.201) on the approximation of the laws, regulations and administrative provisions of the Member States relating to the restrictions on the marketing and use of certain dangerous substances and preparations.

items.title: The European Communities (Designation) Order 2002 (revoked)
items.description: This is a further Order in the series designating authorities which may exercise the power to make regulations conferred by section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 and specifying the matters in relation to which that power may be exercised. The Order also revokes two designations which are superseded, and amends article 2(2) of the European Communities (Designation) (No. 4) Order 2001.

items.title: The Pitcairn Court of Appeal Order 2000 (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order removes inappropriate language from the form of Judicial affirmation in the Pitcairn Court of Appeal Order 2000.

items.title: The Social Security Amendment (Carer’s Allowance) Regulations 2002
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Invalid Care Allowance) Regulations 1976 (S.I. 1976/409) and other Regulations as a consequence of changes made to section 70 of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 by the Regulatory Reform (Carer’s Allowance) Order 2002 (S.I. 2002/1457).

items.title: The Statistics of Trade (Customs and Excise) (Amendment) Regulations 2002 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Statistics of Trade (Customs and Excise) (Amendment) Regulations 2001, an error having occurred in the making of those Regulations.

items.title: The Territorial Sea Act 1987 (Jersey) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order amends the Territorial Sea Act 1987 (Jersey) Order 1997, which extended the 1987 Act to Jersey, to take account of the maritime boundary established between France and Jersey in accordance with an Agreement between the United Kingdom and the French Republic, signed at St Helier on 4 July 2000 (Cm 5024).

items.title: The Al-Qa'ida and Taliban (United Nations Measures) (Amendment) Order 2002
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, amends the Al-Qa'ida and Taliban (United Nations Measures) Order 2002 to correct a drafting error.

items.title: Teacher Training Agency (Additional Functions) (No. 2) (Amendment) Order 2002
